races.race_name: Today's Gate Sponsored By The INPBA Fillies Maiden

runners.name: Star Of Beauty
runners.spotlight: 0-6 and rated 74; some solid efforts in defeat, including a 0.5l third of 13 in a Naas maiden last August (6f, good); shaped like worth another go at 1m when a keeping on second in a 7f claimer at Roscommon latest; player.
runners.comment: 0-6, rated 74, decent run in 7f claimer latest, longer trip here a plus, player
runners.owner: David K Kelly & Mrs Pauline Condon

runners.name: Spring Sprinter
runners.spotlight: Elzaam filly; sister to 1m2f-12.4f Flat (RPR 99) and useful 2m-2m2f hurdle winner Smooth Tom, half-sister to 1m6f Flat (89) and useful 1m7f-2m1f hurdle/chase winner Royal Hollow; from a family the trainer knows well and could go well on debut.
runners.comment: Elzaam filly, from family trainer knows well, could run a big race on debut

runners.name: Elusive Echo
runners.spotlight: Sea The Stars filly, E48,000 foal; sister to winners Rogue King (1m4f AW, RPR 74; 2m1f hurdle) and Motriff (UAE 9.7f), half-sister to 1m/1m2f winner Evident Beauty (99); dam unraced half-sister to 1m2f-1m4f Group 1 winner The Fugue; first of seven in 7f barrier trial at Naas last week; tongue tied for debut; check the market.
runners.comment: Sea The Stars filly, E48,000 foal, dam half-sister to The Fugue, tongue tied, check market

runners.name: Sweetie Too
runners.spotlight: Dawn Approach filly; dam runner-up 6f 2yo (RPR 74), half-sister to winners New Treasure (6f 2yo Group 3) and Regheeb (useful 1m-1m2f), out of 6f 2yo/7f Group 3 winner; a bit of appeal on paper, so check the betting.
runners.comment: Dawn Approach filly, dam half-sister to Group 3 winner, check the betting

runners.name: Black Chicken
runners.spotlight: Sottsass filly; E38,000 yearling; dam 9.4f winner (RPR 72), sister to 1m4f Group 3 winner Pollara, closely related to useful 1m 2yo winner Engage, out of 6f 2yo/8.5f winning half-sister to Arc winner Solemia; market best guide on debut; fourth of 7 in 7f barrier trial at Naas; was behind Elusive Echo then.
runners.comment: E38,000 Sottsass filly, dam 9.4f winner, market best guide on debut

runners.name: Quinchy Lady
runners.spotlight: Alkumait filly, half-sister to six winners inc Chuck Willis (Listed-placed 5f-1m inc 2yo/US/Canadian dirt; RPR 102), Imaginarium (1m1f Flat, 96; 2m hurdle) and Xebec (1m2f-1m4f; 95); dam 7f winner (inc 2yo/AW; 79); likely best watched on debut unless the market speaks.
runners.comment: Alkumait filly, dam 7f winner, likely best watched on debut unless market speaks

runners.name: Glasarmi
runners.spotlight: Not a bad effort over 7f (yielding) here on debut, showing up well before weakening into a 12l fifth of 12; not sure this longer trip will suit on that evidence.
runners.comment: Weakened into 12l fifth over 7f here on debut, not sure this extra furlong will suit
runners.owner: P R Dowling & Ms G Brennan

runners.name: Ballerina Dancer
runners.spotlight: Massive odds and has failed to beat a rival in maidens at Leopardstown and Limerick; look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Huge odds and ran accordingly in both starts, look elsewhere

runners.name: Flagstone Lady
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten at long odds in maidens at Cork and over C&D; will qualify for handicaps after this.
runners.comment: No impact in two maidens at long odds, handicaps beckon after this

runners.name: Kirkland Sioux
runners.spotlight: Sioux Nation filly has failed to make an impact in maidens at Leopardstown (1m, good) and when 66-1 at Dundalk on latest (7f); others preferred.
runners.comment: Sioux Nation filly, soundly beaten in 7f-1m maidens, others preferred

runners.name: Amenita
runners.spotlight: Kingman filly didn't show much in sole start last season at Cork (7f, soft) when slowly away; pedigree suggests she'll get better with age and she's open to plenty of improvement on her seasonal reappearance.
runners.comment: No impact in sole start last year, bred to get better with age, could improve plenty

races.race_name: KCLR 96FM Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Royal County Glory
runners.spotlight: Scored off 3lb lower at Dundalk (7f) on penultimate; came up short on return to turf when a 3l fifth off revised mark at Fairyhouse (7f, good); eased 2lb and worth a go at 1m on that evidence; place shout.
runners.comment: Won off 3lb lower at Dundalk on penultimate, 1m could suit on evidence of Fairyhouse fifth

runners.name: Queen Aethelflaed
runners.spotlight: Won off 1lb lower at Thurles last October (1m, gd-yld), her last start in this grade; soundly beaten both starts this term but could revive back in 0-60 company.
runners.comment: Won last start in this grade at Thurles, poor so far this season but could revive

runners.name: Heart Of Darkness
runners.spotlight: AW winner is 0-10 on turf and yet to trouble the judge; best efforts on grass have come on heavy ground, so the forecast rain is a plus, but others appeal more.
runners.comment: 0-10 on turf, best efforts on testing ground, forecast rain a plus but others preferred
runners.owner: B O'Sullivan/D Whyte/Exors of the Late D McHugh/M O'Loghlen

runners.name: Hale Bopp
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner; 2-33 on turf but hasn't won a race of any description since September 2023; decent third over C&D latest, however, and place claims if he can back that up.
runners.comment: No win since 2023, decent third over C&D latest, place chance if backs that up

runners.name: Rock Etoile
runners.spotlight: Last turf win came over C&D in September 2024 off 18lb higher; finished last year in poor form, however, and no signs of a revival in two runs this term.
runners.comment: Last turf win came over C&D off 18lb higher, way below best in two runs this term
runners.owner: Conor McKenna & Patrick J McKenna & Emmet McKenna

runners.name: Chou Chou
runners.spotlight: Two wins last year over slightly further than this at Tipperary (gd-fm) and Clonmel (soft); remains 3lb above that last success and tailed off over hurdles at Killarney 23 days ago.
runners.comment: 3lb above last win at Clonmel, tailed off over hurdles at Killarney 23 days ago

runners.name: Balance Of Trade
runners.spotlight: 0-10; best form on soft and heavy ground; ran well in C&D claimer last month (soft) and again in a Bellewstown handicap (1m, soft) on penultimate but poor on quicker ground at Ballinrobe latest; rain would boost chance.
runners.comment: 0-10, best form on soft and heavy ground, needs rain, chance if it arrives

runners.name: Believeth
runners.spotlight: Didn't show much in maidens at 6f-1m on the AW and on good to firm ground; weakened out of it on handicap debut at Leopardstown (1m, gd-yld); 5lb drop not enough to tempt; tongue tie applied.
runners.comment: Modest maiden form, similar story on h'cap debut latest, eased 5lb, tongue tie on,

runners.name: Gold Dublooms
runners.spotlight: Ran well when a 5.5l third over C&D (good) on handicap debut last May and 9lb lower now; off since 10l defeat at Dundalk in January; a repeat of that C&D run last season would give him a squeak.
runners.comment: Good run on handicap debut over C&D last May, a repeat of that would give him a squeak

runners.name: Tynamite
runners.spotlight: Veteran is a six-time AW winner but 0-33 on turf; no success since December 2023 and never involved on seasonal return at Killarney 20 days ago; others preferred.
runners.comment: Veteran, 0-33 on turf, never involved on seasonal return at Killarney, others preferred

runners.name: Brains
runners.spotlight: Veteran has shaped very nicely the last twice at Leopardstown (7f, yld-sft) and Roscommon (7.5f, good); close third off 3lb higher over C&D last summer; big chance with the step back up to 1m sure to suit.
runners.comment: Fine efforts the last twice, placed off 3lb higher over C&D last summer, big chance

runners.name: Real Petite
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose maiden; tailed off when last of 19 on return to the Flat at Leopardstown over 1m5f; big drop in trip now; impossible to fancy.
runners.comment: Dual-purpose maiden, tailed off back on Flat on latest, impossible to fancy

runners.name: Medieval Night
runners.spotlight: Placed on handicap debut for Dermot Weld last August off 6lb higher but regressive form since; beaten 21l over 1m6f here latest; drops in trip; hard to fancy on recent form; cheekpieces tried.
runners.comment: Gone backwards since promising h'cap debut, beaten 21l latest, cheekpieces tried

runners.name: Monastere
runners.spotlight: No sign of winning potential in maidens or in first two handicaps; yard in hot form though and still early days, so worth monitoring in the market.
runners.comment: No sign of winning potential but still early days, yard in hot form, check the market

runners.name: Half Nutz
runners.spotlight: Six wins but losing run stretches back to September 2022; not terrible efforts over C&D the last twice, but needs to find more to get his head back in front.
runners.comment: No win since Sept' 2022, okay C&D efforts the last twice, needs more

runners.name: Pinball Wizard
runners.spotlight: A winner of six races when trained in Britain but failed to hit the frame for this stable last season; on a dangerous mark but lacks a run and likely best watched.
runners.comment: Six-time winner in Britain, poor for this yard last year, lacks a run, likely best watched

races.race_name: Sonix Entertainment Handicap

runners.name: Beauparc
runners.spotlight: Won this last year off 3lb lower; in good form on AW earlier this year but underwhelming return to turf when beaten 7l at Sligo (10.5f, yld-sft) on latest; return to 1m4f a plus and Colin Keane takes over; should go well.
runners.comment: Won this last year off 3lb lower, trip/ground excuse latest, Keane up, should go well

runners.name: Navinsky
runners.spotlight: Placed in one of four starts in maidens, at Navan last September (1m2f) when a neck runner up; well below that level on seasonal return in Ballinrobe maiden; steps up in trip for handicap debut; check the market.
runners.comment: Placed in 1m2f Navan maiden last season, moves up in trip for h'cap bow, check market

runners.name: Flying Fortress
runners.spotlight: Ground-versatile sort won a Ballinrobe maiden (9.5f, good) and a Listowel handicap (8.5f, heavy) last season off 1lb lower; modest effort on seasonal return at Ballinrobe (9.5f, gd-yld) but sharper for that and can't be discounted with yard in form.
runners.comment: Won Listowel handicap off 1lb lower last season, ground versatile, yard in form

runners.name: Tatum
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; enjoyed an excellent campaign last season which included two wins at Leopardstown (1m4f, good and 1m5f, good); 1m2f too sharp on return there latest and could do better now stepped back up in trip.
runners.comment: Two wins at Leopardstown last year, 1m2f too sharp on latest, do better now; reserve

runners.name: Daboya
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose; just 1lb higher than when winning a Curragh handicap last season (1m4f, good); shaped with promise when a 5l fifth of 14 at Roscommon latest and has gone well here before; interesting but wouldn't want to see much rain.
runners.comment: Won off 1lb lower last year, shaped nicely at Roscommon latest, has gone well here before

runners.name: Bobby Kirova
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; mild promise in maidens but has bombed out in first two handicap starts here (9.5f, soft) and at Roscommon (1m4f, good); eased 4lb but others preferred.
runners.comment: Mild promise in maidens, bombed out in first two handicaps, others preferred; reserve

runners.name: Just Keep Flying
runners.spotlight: Won a Goodwood maiden (1m2f, gd-fm) on debut for the Gosdens; creditable efforts on turf and AW in three subsequent starts; bought for 20,000gns in December; overlooked by Foley, so probably best watched after break on stable bow.
runners.comment: Goodwood winner for Gosdens, bought for 20,000gns, overlooked by Foley on yard debut

runners.name: Obscenity
runners.spotlight: In fine form this season; third on seasonal return at Bellewstown (11.5f, soft); won by 5.5l at Navan (1m2f, gd-yld); hiked up 12lb but again ran well when a 2.75l runner up at Roscommon latest (1m4f, yielding) when not getting the clearest of runs; in the mix again off 1lb higher.
runners.comment: In fine form, raised 12lb for Navan win, good second off revised mark latest, in the mix

runners.name: Inthesmallhours
runners.spotlight: Dundalk maiden winner in December; has beaten just one rival home in first two turf handicaps at Sligo (10.5f, soft) and Leopardstown (1m2f, good); yard going well but she's hard to fancy on those last two efforts.
runners.comment: Dundalk maiden winner, struggled in first two turf handicaps, hard to fancy for now

runners.name: Stellar Quality
runners.spotlight: Bellewstown maiden winner on second start last August (12.5f, good); modest effort on seasonal return in six-runner Killarney race; the form of that Bellewstown race has worked out and she could go well on handicap debut; preferred by Foley.
runners.comment: Bellewstown maiden winner, form has worked out, could go well on handicap debut

runners.name: Voice Of Reason
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose; 1-9 on turf on the Flat; won on the AW (1m4f) off 4lb lower in January and has generally held form well since; hasn't won on turf since 2023, however, and others make more appeal.
runners.comment: 1-9 on turf, AW win in January, no win on the grass since 2023, others make more appeal

runners.name: Sir Callisto
runners.spotlight: In fine form over 1m4f on the AW since return from a break; won off 72 in early April and close second off revised mark later that month; raised another 3lb for that but can't be discounted on return to turf (won on good ground in France).
runners.comment: In fine form on AW, won in early April, close second latest, up 3lb but can't discount

runners.name: Justenzia
runners.spotlight: Sole win off 16lb lower at Clonmel last September (9.5f, soft); runner up both starts this term, including here over 9.5f (soft); 2l behind Satono Chevalier at Ballinrobe on latest (1m5f, gd-yld); 4lb better off now and could turn the tables; forecast rain a plus.
runners.comment: 4lb swing for 2l defeat to Satono Chevalier latest, rain a plus, could turn tables

runners.name: Spanish Rising
runners.spotlight: Placed in a 1m2f Ripon maiden (good) for former yard; soundly beaten on debut for this stable at the Curragh (1m, heavy); worth a market check on handicap debut, though rain would be a concern.
runners.comment: H'cap debutant, placed in Ripon maiden last year, poor on heavy latest, rain a worry

runners.name: Not Just Any Eagle
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner; last scored at Leopardstown in May 2025 off 4lb lower; struggled on soft ground on seasonal return over 1m6f here in April; might need another run or two to hit top form.
runners.comment: Five-time winner, 4lb above last winning mark, may need another run or two to hit top form

runners.name: Cala Bonita
runners.spotlight: Won back-to-back races at Dundalk last autumn (both 10.5f); also won a 1m handicap here last season (good) off 14lb lower; off since November and stamina to prove for this trip, so probably best watched.
runners.comment: Won over 1m here off 14lb lower last season, lacks a recent run, stamina to prove

runners.name: Derry Lad
runners.spotlight: First reserve; goes on any ground but on losing run since a Pontefract win in June 2023; well treated on that form and not disgraced when a rallying 7.25l fifth at Leopardstown latest (1m4f); place shout if backs that up.
runners.comment: Ground versatile, on long losing run, ok run at Leopardstown latest, place shout; reserve

runners.name: Satono Chevalier
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner is 3-14 on turf; has notched up back-to-back wins at Navan (1m2f, heavy) and on debut for this yard at Ballinrobe (1m5f, gd-yld); has gone up 14lb but has won off 2lb higher on the AW, so still on a feasible mark.
runners.comment: Raised 14lb for back-to-back wins at Navan and Ballinrobe, has won off higher mark on AW

runners.name: Pavilion End
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose AW winner; out of form at Dundalk earlier this year and disappointing return to turf at the Curragh eight days ago; eased 1lb and down in trip, but others arrive in better fettle.
runners.comment: AW winner, disappointing return to turf at the Curragh, others arrive in better form

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap

runners.name: River Ara
runners.spotlight: Made all in back-end 1m nursery at Leopardstown (yielding to soft); unable to land a blow in 7f Guineas Trial at same venue last month, but finished not far off some useful types; sights lowered now after a Listed run at Naas; cheekpieces fitted; sole filly of her age in this race.
runners.comment: Nursery winner, highly tied on first two starts this term, respected in this grade
runners.owner: Westerberg & Mrs Magnier & M Tabor & D Smith

runners.name: Pink Oxalis
runners.spotlight: Four wins last year, including over 1m at this track on soft in April; career-best when landing a very competitive fillies' handicap at Leopardstown last September; not in the same form on first two starts this year; slowly away over 7f here last time, staying-on third was a decent effort in the circumstances.
runners.comment: Four wins last year, highlight at Leopardstown, third here on latest was reassuring

runners.name: Thrifty Of Digby
runners.spotlight: Two wins last year, including over 1m at this venue; two moderate runs this term, including in a Sligo race in which Chico Guerrera was second, before returning to form with a winning display at Roscommon a fortnight ago; made all over the extended 7f then, this trip is within her range; 5lb extra does not seem harsh.
runners.comment: Made all at Roscommon, this longer trip is within her range, 5lb extra does not seem harsh

runners.name: Ob La Di
runners.spotlight: Won over an extended 1m2f at Naas last August on good ground; not suited by heavy ground on her final two starts; in rear on soft on return; much better when fourth at Leopardstown, 1 3/4l in front of favourite Akecheta.
runners.comment: Improved display when fourth at Leopardstown, 1 3/4l in front of favourite Akecheta then

runners.name: Chica Guerrera
runners.spotlight: Tramore 1m4f winner last August and finished the season well with placed efforts in big fields on heavy ground at the Curragh; Sligo second early last month was on very soft ground; prominent all the way before fading into fourth over 1m2f on good ground at Leopardstown last time.
runners.comment: Fading into fourth over 1m2f at Leopardstown last time, possibly best on soft ground

runners.name: Akecheta
runners.spotlight: Gained second heavy-ground win in a conditions race at this venue (7f) last September; yet to win a handicap; well supported in three of her four races this term, including the Irish Lincoln; has not run to expectations; new headgear combination tried.
runners.comment: Well supported in three of her four races this term, has not run to expectations

runners.name: Spanish Temptress
runners.spotlight: Went up an aggregate 21lb for three handicap wins last season; seemed to be improving when second at Limerick in April, beaten only a nose; Curragh run eight days ago (17th of 19) was too bad to be true; 7-2 chance then, interesting to see if the market is prepared to overlook that showing.
runners.comment: Went up an aggregate 21lb for three handicap wins last season, had an off day on latest

runners.name: Tachos
runners.spotlight: C&D winner in 2024; added another over 1m1f at Leopardstown last June; ran creditably when fifth behind today's rival Pink Oxalis at Leopardstown last September; out of her depth in a Listed race at Dundalk on final start last season.
runners.comment: Fifth behind today's rival Pink Oxalis at Leopardstown last September, lacks a recent run

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Gowran Classic (Smullen Series)

runners.name: Hardy Warrior
runners.spotlight: Took second behind Derby candidate Benvenuto Cellini in the four-runner Futurity Stakes at the Curragh last August; fourth to Puerto Rico in Group 1 at Saint-Cloud in October; won a three-runner 1m conditions event at Naas on reappearance; did not quite see out the extended 1m2f when third in the Prix Noailles at Longchamp; heads the stable's strong challenge.
runners.comment: High-class colt who has run creditably in Pattern company, heads stable's strong challenge

runners.name: Summer Is Tomorrow
runners.spotlight: Looked well suited by 7f when winning twice at two; faced a stiff task when the outsider of four in a conditions race at Cork on seasonal debut; second-best of the Joseph O'Brien squad on official figures, 3lb ahead of Perisher.
runners.comment: Looked well suited by 7f at two, faced stiff task on seasonal debut, place chance at best

runners.name: Killashee Warrior
runners.spotlight: Thurles maiden winner last October after a fine effort in a 7f Listed race at Leopardstown; made a very pleasing start to the season when fourth behind True Love in a Group 3 event at Leopardstown; not quite as promising in the same category at the Curragh last time; fair prospects of reaching the money.
runners.comment: Made a very pleasing start to the season at Leopardstown, not quite as promising last time

runners.name: Perisher
runners.spotlight: Winning favourite on his last two starts, in a 1m maiden at this venue and over a longer trip in a Naas conditions event; up 11lb but still 16lb inferior to stablemate Hardy Warrior
runners.comment: Winning favourite on his last two starts, up 11lb but still 16lb inferior to Hardy Warrior

runners.name: Brownstown
runners.spotlight: Kept good company last season; ran twice in Pattern races, also contested a strong Listed race and the Goffs Million; 90-rated filly has a place chance if she can improve a little from her reappearance in a conditions race at Cork.
runners.comment: Kept good company last season, place chance if she can improve from reappearance at Cork

runners.name: Bunyola Bay
runners.spotlight: Finished third of eight, beaten 3l, when the well-backed favourite for a soft-ground maiden at Newbury last October (winner finished fourth in the Greenham); made all for 7f Doncaster win on seasonal debut; should stay and could be progressive
runners.comment: Ran once at two, made all for 7f Doncaster win in April, should stay, could be progressive

runners.name: Flawless Fusion
runners.spotlight: 75-rated maiden filly from the same stable as the 107-rated colt Hardy Warrior; appears to be making up the numbers.
runners.comment: 75-rated maiden filly from the same stable as the 107-rated colt Hardy Warrior

runners.name: Mo Mhuirin
runners.spotlight: Long-priced winner of a 1m maiden at Dundalk last December; satisfactory run at Killarney on turf/seasonal debut; tough task in this company
runners.comment: AW maiden winner, satisfactory run on turf/seasonal debut, tough task in this company

runners.name: Camino Vino
runners.spotlight: Beat an 86-rated previous winner in a 1m contest at Dundalk in December on her only juvenile start; set a tough task in a fillies' Group 3 event at Navan on return; can improve
runners.comment: Won a 1m maiden on AW last year, set a tough task at Navan on return, can improve

runners.name: Karrygrant
runners.spotlight: Beaten favourite in maidens at Bellewstown and Roscommon on his two most recent starts; not suited by race conditions.
runners.comment: Beaten favourite in maidens on his two most recent starts, not suited by race conditions

runners.name: Cherry Hill Girl
runners.spotlight: Maiden win at two was achieved on heavy ground; no appeal on this year's handicap form; rated 4lb below her less exposed stablemate Mo Mhuirin.
runners.comment: Maiden win at two was achieved on heavy ground, no appeal on this year's handicap form

runners.name: Manton Bay
runners.spotlight: AW maiden winner last year; has shown a fair standard of form in turf handicaps; needs to improve to feature here
runners.comment: AW maiden winner, fair standard of form in turf handicaps, needs to improve to feature now

runners.name: Marjorie Daw
runners.spotlight: Course 1m maiden winner on heavy in October; well held in fourth behind Perisher at Naas last month; unlikely to make an impact in this company.
runners.comment: Well held in fourth behind Perisher at Naas, unlikely to make an impact in this company
runners.owner: T O'Sullivan & S R Stafford & Crampscastle Bloodstock Ltd

runners.name: Miss Romagna
runners.spotlight: Looks a possible future maiden winner on the strength of Cork second; hard to fancy at this level; second reserve.
runners.comment: Second reserve, looks a possible future maiden winner, hard to fancy at this level

runners.name: Honouramongthieves
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in two handicaps; 73-rated gelding looks out of his depth; cheekpieces fitted now; first reserve.
runners.comment: First reserve, unplaced in two handicaps, 73-rated gelding looks out of his depth

runners.name: Satoyama
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner over 1m at this venue on his second start; unable to reward market support at Listowel yesterday, but ran with credit in fourth.
runners.comment: Maiden winner over 1m at this venue, due to run in the 3.50 at Listowel on Sunday

runners.name: Matilda Joslyngage
runners.spotlight: Stayed on well into second behind Killashee Warrior at Thurles last October; another solid run on seasonal debut; will not need to improve much to win a race, but this is likely to prove much too demanding.
runners.comment: Will not need to improve much to win a race but this is likely to prove much too demanding

runners.name: Louisiana
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in fourth on only start; huge task in a race of this nature; third reserve.
runners.comment: Third reserve, soundly beaten in fourth on only start, huge task in a race of this nature

runners.name: Loch Gamhna
runners.spotlight: Best effort came on second start last season when third behind the talented Causeway in a 7f Curragh maiden; overall form suggests he faces a huge task at these weights.
runners.comment: Best run was over 7f on heavy ground last season, faces a huge task at these weights

races.race_name: QuinnBet (C & G) Maiden

runners.name: Changing Lanes
runners.spotlight: Winning potential in both starts; beaten 1.75l on debut at Dundalk (1m4f); neck runner up over 10.5f (gd-yld) at Roscommon latest; cheekpieces go on; leading chance.
runners.comment: Fine runs in defeat at Dundalk and Roscommon, cheekpieces go on, leading chance

runners.name: Listentodwindblow
runners.spotlight: Rated 92 after five starts; respectable runs in Goffs Million and valuable auction series final at Naas; 6.75l sixth of 8 in Leopardstown Group 3 in October; beaten favourite on seasonal return at the Curragh (7f, heavy); gelded since; big chance on the figures but stamina to prove; due to run 3.50 Listowel on Saturday.
runners.comment: Rated 92, beaten fav' over 7f on return at Curragh, gelded since, stamina to prove

runners.name: Cracking Cloud
runners.spotlight: Excellent effort over C&D (soft) on debut last month; didn't back it up on better ground at Ballinrobe (9.5f, gd-yld); could rebound if the forecast rain materialises.
runners.comment: Fine effort over C&D on debut on soft, not as good latest, could rebound if rain arrives

runners.name: Pinoroyale
runners.spotlight: Pinatubo gelding; E75,000 yearling; closely related to 7f-1m4f winner Golden Lyra (inc 2yo/AW/Listed; RPR 109), half-brother to winners Going Gone (1m2f/1m4f; 103) and Sir Callisto (9.5f turf/1m4f AW; 84); dam unraced sister to German Derby winner Sea The Moon; plenty to like on paper; market can guide.
runners.comment: Pinatubo gelding, dam unraced sister to Sea The Moon, market can guide on debut

runners.name: Sharkeyboy
runners.spotlight: Promise when fifth on debut at the Curragh (1m1f, yld-sft) last September; denied by a head at Leopardstown when last seen in October and the fourth from that race has since gone close in a Group 3; fascinating runner for champion jumps trainer.
runners.comment: Promise both runs last year, form of latest second boosted, fascinating runner

runners.name: Confused
runners.spotlight: Calyx gelding was a respectable fourth of 16 on debut at Cork (10.5f, gd-yld) when sent off at 6-1; didn't quite get home so this shorter trip is a plus; likely to improve and could go well.
runners.comment: Respectable run on debut at Cork when 6-1, drop in trip here a plus, could go well

runners.name: Parish Team
runners.spotlight: Home-bred Parish Hall gelding; dam unraced sister to 1m2f winner Ambitious Approach, out of once-raced half-sister to 2m-3m hurdle/chase winner Istimraar; worth a market check on debut.
runners.comment: Parish Hall gelding, dam sister to 1m2f winner, worth a market check on debut

runners.name: Jumpingthegun
runners.spotlight: 100-1 and hooded (retained) on debut at Navan (1m, heavy) but wasn't disgraced, beaten 11l into seventh of 15; this longer trip should suit but more needed.
runners.comment: 100-1 on debut but run not devoid of promise, longer trip a plus but more needed

runners.name: The Austrian
runners.spotlight: New Bay gelding; half-brother to winners Snowcapped (Listed-placed 7f/1m; RPR 101) and It's Snowing (6f AW 2yo; 77); dam once-raced (35) half-sister to Oaks/Irish Oaks winner Snow Fairy; worth a market check on debut.
runners.comment: New Bay gelding, dam half-sister to top class racemare Snow Fairy, check market

runners.name: Will You Stop
runners.spotlight: Saxon Warrior gelding; kept on nicely on debut here (1m, gd-yld) when a 14-1 shot; looks well worth a go at this longer trip and could challenge for minor money if building on that debut effort.
runners.comment: Kept on well over 1m here on debut, moves up in trip, could challenge for minor money

runners.name: If Not For Grace
runners.spotlight: Elzaam gelding; brother to winners Mjjack (6f-1m2f. RPR 115) and Almustawrad (KSA 7f/1m1f), half-brother to four winners inc Chiara Wells (Italian 5f-6f inc Listed; 95); dam placed 5f 2yo (57); likely to improve for this initial experience.
runners.comment: Elzaam gelding, related to plenty of winners, improve for this initial experience

runners.name: Footstepstoglory
runners.spotlight: Runner up in Limerick handicap hurdle when last seen in October; related to Flat winners so worth a market check on debut in this sphere.
runners.comment: Runner up over hurdles in October, off since, related to Flat winners, check market

runners.name: Royal Sapphire
runners.spotlight: 0-6 and has looked limited; heavy defeats in claimers the last twice; impossible to make a case for.
runners.comment: 0-6, has looked limited, heavy defeats in claimers last twice, hard to make a case for

runners.name: Hello Garda
runners.spotlight: Huge odds and well beaten in 1m maidens here on latest and at Cork last October; looks one for handicaps in due course.
runners.comment: Huge odds and well beaten in 1m maidens here and at Naas, looks one for handicaps

runners.name: It's Good To Talk
runners.spotlight: Runner up in a heavy ground bumper at Naas on debut in February; beaten 26l on Flat debut in Roscommon maiden (10.5f, gd-yld) latest; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Bumper runner up on heavy, soundly beaten on Flat debut latest, others appeal more

runners.name: Hugo Spritz
runners.spotlight: 125-1 and failed to beat a rival on debut at Dundalk in March; unlikely to be the answer here.
runners.comment: 125-1 and failed to beat a rival on debut at Dundalk in March, unlikely

runners.name: Silverhillsox
runners.spotlight: 125-1 and ran accordingly on debut at the Curragh; seems safe to rule out on that evidence.
runners.comment: 125-1 and ran accordingly on debut at the Curragh, safe to rule out

races.race_name: Ladies Day July 25th Handicap (Div I)

runners.name: Pliny
runners.spotlight: Has dropped a long way in the ratings; well exposed but has a decent chance in a race of this standard, especially since he finished in front of Accelereight (improved last time) at Navan in March.
runners.comment: Has dropped a long way in the ratings, well exposed but has a decent chance in this grade

runners.name: Onyeisi
runners.spotlight: Interesting on his AW form for Charlie Johnston; poor run on Irish debut at Roscommon; stable's good current form is a positive.
runners.comment: Interesting on his AW form for Charlie Johnston, stable's good current form is a positive

runners.name: Accelereight
runners.spotlight: Modest form in maidens; similar story on seasonal return and handicap debut at Navan (1m2f, heavy) in March; promising second at Ballinrobe, seemingly appreciating better ground; closely matched with Pliny on Navan run; holds an obvious chance, with the form of the Ballinrobe race working out well
runners.comment: Promising second at Bellewstown, closely matched with Pliny on Navan run, obvious chance
runners.owner: Any Excuse For A Holiday Syndicate

runners.name: Roaming Coyote
runners.spotlight: Maiden form not good enough to generate confidence for this first handicap attempt.
runners.comment: Maiden form not good enough to generate confidence for this first handicap attempt

runners.name: Cadreville
runners.spotlight: Nothing of merit in his maiden form; towards the rear at Navan in his first handicap. considerable improvement required.
runners.comment: Towards the rear at Navan in his first handicap, considerable improvement required

runners.name: Music And Song
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to five winners from 7f to 1m6f, several of them useful; weak form at Dundalk and well beaten at Sligo in her first handicap, blinkers tried now
runners.comment: Weak form at Dundalk and well beaten at Sligo in her first handicap, blinkers tried now

runners.name: Dawson Street
runners.spotlight: Modest form in nurseries at Dundalk; finished with only one behind him in the Bellewstown race in which Accelereight was second; first time with blinkers.
runners.comment: Finished with only one behind him in the Bellewstown race in which Accelereight was second
runners.owner: Roderick Ryan & Mrs John M Weld

runners.name: Kart Of Gold
runners.spotlight: Remote last of nine finishers at Sligo last month in his first handicap (Pliny, third); seems safe to rule out.
runners.comment: Remote last of nine finishers at Sligo in his first handicap, seems safe to rule out

runners.name: Swinging The World
runners.spotlight: Ran in strong maidens; down the field in a nursery at Naas last September; gelded now and has changed stable.
runners.comment: Ran in strong maidens, down the field in a nursery, gelded now and has changed stable

runners.name: Private Larry
runners.spotlight: Weak maiden form, last of 13 on heavy ground at Navan on his only handicap start; has been gelded and now hooded for the first time
runners.comment: Weak maiden form, last of 13 on heavy ground at Navan on his only handicap start, hooded

runners.name: Connova
runners.spotlight: Last year's maiden form does not encourage support; should be more mature now; dam did not show much but has a decent pedigree.
runners.comment: Last year's maiden form does not encourage support, should at least be more mature now

runners.name: Sweet Baby Zou
runners.spotlight: No sign of winning potential in three qualifying runs at 6f-1m2f on testing ground; mid-field on handicap debut at Roscommon, not beaten far; beaten favourite at Down Royal on Friday, 7f looked inadequate then, may do better now if cheekpieces do the trick
runners.comment: Beaten favourite at Down Royal on Friday, 7f looked inadequate then, may do better now

runners.name: Minella Buoy
runners.spotlight: Modest juvenile form; his chance depends on his ability to improve on his first run on good ground.
runners.comment: Modest juvenile, chance depends on his ability to improve on his first run on good ground

runners.name: Jewels Legacy
runners.spotlight: Hard to fancy on juvenile form and lacks the advantage of a recent run; up against it.
runners.comment: Hard to fancy on juvenile form and lacks the advantage of a recent run, up against it

races.race_name: Ladies Day July 25th Handicap (Div II)

runners.name: Neon Eyes
runners.spotlight: Three 7f runs at Dundalk over the winter, best of them when placed on debut; noted staying on in a 1m contest at this track on her turf/handicap debut; has a slight edge over Ochtar Againn; definite chance.
runners.comment: Noted staying on in a 1m contest at this track on her handicap debut, definite chance

runners.name: Borora Aura
runners.spotlight: Third placing in a Ballinrobe claimer last month was a step in the right direction; more needed.
runners.comment: Third placing in a Ballinrobe claimer was a step in the right direction, more needed

runners.name: Amrum
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to Irish 2,000 Guineas winner Siskin; has looked limited in maidens; a bit better at Down Royal on Friday; quick reappearance is potentially significant; may be competitive at this modest level.
runners.comment: A bit better at Down Royal on Friday, well-related colt may be competitive at this level

runners.name: Starflow
runners.spotlight: Has shown very little in maidens but the stable's current form (Flat and jumps) provides some encouragement for his prospects in his first handicap.
runners.comment: Has shown very little in maidens but the stable's current form provides some encouragement

runners.name: A Pretty Penny
runners.spotlight: Hints of ability at Dundalk during the winter; showed improved form to win when raised in trip at Ballinrobe; up 6lb now, strong contender whose chance could be advertised by Ballinrobe runner-up Accelereight in the first heat of this contest; the form of the Ballinrobe race already looks solid.
runners.comment: Showed improved form to win when raised in trip at Ballinrobe, up 6lb, strong contender

runners.name: Rodeo Blues
runners.spotlight: Three moderate displays in maidens over shorter trips; may be competitive in this grade for a top stable.
runners.comment: Three moderate displays in maidens, may be competitive in this grade for a top stable
runners.owner: Annus Mirabilis Synd'/Sean O'Driscoll/Middle Cow Unlimited

runners.name: Concaire
runners.spotlight: Signs of improvement on handicap debut when sixth of 14 at Roscommon three weeks ago; trip and ground should suit.
runners.comment: Signs of improvement on handicap debut at Roscommon, trip and ground should suit

runners.name: Wedding Year
runners.spotlight: Showed next to nothing in three turf maidens and a nursery; improved when switched to AW action at Dundalk; could be in the mix here if progress transfers to turf.
runners.comment: Low-rated filly has prospects of picking up a race at some stage, judged on Dundalk form

runners.name: Premier Princess
runners.spotlight: Maiden form is unconvincing; feasible that she will find her level in this grade but needs to show a spark of improvement.
runners.comment: Maiden form is unconvincing, feasible that she will find her level in this grade

runners.name: Ochtar Againn
runners.spotlight: Shaped quite well over 1m at this venue on handicap debut; not much between her and fourth-placed Neon Eyes; extra distance here should help.
runners.comment: Shaped quite well over 1m at this venue on handicap debut, extra distance should help

runners.name: Zitkala Sa
runners.spotlight: Does not look well handicapped on the evidence of three runs over longer distances; cheekpieces now added to tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Does not look well handicapped on the evidence of three runs over longer distances

runners.name: Maker's Mark
runners.spotlight: Towards the back in two nurseries last season; down in the weights, Sadhbh Tormey's useful 7lb claim will help.
runners.comment: Towards the back in two nurseries last season, down in the weights, 7lb claim will help
runners.owner: Hugh J O'Brien & Colm O'Brien & Ms Denise McCourt

runners.name: Threebiggulps
runners.spotlight: Weak form in maidens over shorter trips; rider's useful claim is the only obvious positive.
runners.comment: Weak form in maidens over shorter trips, rider's useful claim is the only obvious positive

runners.name: Curious Jane
runners.spotlight: Has finished in front of only four of her 36 rivals in three races; hard to make a case for her.
runners.comment: Has finished in front of only four of her 36 rivals in three races, hard to make a case

runners.name: Canon Law
runners.spotlight: Ran well at a big price over 7f at this track on only nursery start; open to improvement and perhaps the addition of cheekpieces will help.
runners.comment: Ran well at a big price over 7f at this track on only nursery start, open to improvement

races.race_name: Nora Canty Mares Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Salsinha
runners.spotlight: Won good-ground bumper on last summer's debut; good level of form in four starts over hurdles; third in Listed contest at Thurles before Christmas; fourth when odds-on at Fairyhouse in April; little wrong with her second to 122-rated foe at Punchestown; tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: Good level of form in four starts over hurdles; second to 122-rated foe at Punchestown

runners.name: Poetisa
runners.spotlight: Cost plenty after winning a good-ground bumper at Cheltenham last April; turned over at 4-7 on stable/hurdling debut in January; reversed form with the winner when second to highly talented Zanoosh in Listed contest at Navan (2m4f, heavy); returned to bumpers then, second to useful mare at Naas, beaten a long way against geldings last time.
runners.comment: Second to highly talented Zanoosh in Listed contest at Navan (2m4f, heavy) in February

runners.name: Hillhead Runner
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner for Peter Fahey and placed three times in summer maiden hurdles last year for Gavin Cromwell; after a long layoff, well beaten on stable debut at Cork.
runners.comment: Bumper winner was placed three times in summer maiden hurdles last year; well held at Cork

runners.name: Pure Logic
runners.spotlight: Second in three bumpers and a Ballinrobe maiden hurdle; well held on return at Killarney; headgear.
runners.comment: Second in three bumpers and a maiden hurdle; well held on return at Killarney; headgear

runners.name: Wonderful Everyday
runners.spotlight: Did well to beat the geldings on debut in last year's Goffs Defender Bumper at Punchestown; has contested black-type bumpers since, well beaten three times but second in Listed contest at Fairyhouse two runs back; closely related to 2m3f hurdle winner and Townend's choice over the more experienced Poetisa.
runners.comment: Smart if inconsistent bumper performer and Townend's choice over stablemate Poetisa

runners.name: Fraher Field
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner last summer but tailed off in both hurdle outings.
runners.comment: Bumper winner last summer but tailed off in both hurdle outings
runners.owner: John J Flynn, Pierce Flynn, Conor Lannen

runners.name: Bethpage Theatre
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to useful 2m1f-2m6f hurdle/chase winner Drumgill; 100-1 for debut at Cork, fell halfway.
runners.comment: Half-sister to useful 2m1f-2m6f hurdle/chase winner Drumgill; 100-1 at Cork, fell halfway

runners.name: Kauto Empress
runners.spotlight: Robin Of Navan 5yo; dam unraced sister to French 2m hurdle/2m1f chase winner Kauto Sirtaki, out of maiden sister to outstanding chaser Kauto Star.
runners.comment: Robin Of Navan 5yo is from the family of outstanding chaser Kauto Star

runners.name: Misty Valentine
runners.spotlight: Second newcomer for yard; half-sister to winners Handmedownastar (point/2m3f hurdle) and Aleish Lass (point/2m6f hurdle); dam unraced sister to point winner Shalltoo, half-sister to smart 2m4f-3m hurdle/chase winner Dedigout, out of bumper winner.
runners.comment: Second newcomer for yard; ; half-sister to a couple of hurdle winners

runners.name: The Fog Horn
runners.spotlight: Dual 7f winner; huge prices and well beaten in two hurdle runs.
runners.comment: Dual 7f winner; huge prices and well beaten in two hurdle runs

runners.name: Tumbling In
runners.spotlight: Sister to 134-rated hurdler Dream On Baby showed potential in two bumpers in early autumn; will come for this, but not ruled out.
runners.comment: Sister to 134-rated hurdler Dream On Baby showed potential in two bumpers in early autumn
runners.owner: Mrs Paul Shanahan & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Shanliss Tricks
runners.spotlight: Beaten 54l in both hurdle starts.
runners.comment: Beaten 54l in both hurdle starts

runners.name: Rose Mountain
runners.spotlight: Big prices and well held in two bumpers.
runners.comment: Big prices and well held in two bumpers

runners.name: Colugo
runners.spotlight: Modest maiden on the level; keen-goer needs this run to get a handicap mark.
runners.comment: Modest maiden on the level; keen-goer needs this run to get a handicap mark

races.race_name: Southern Waste Management Mares Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Diamond Tipp
runners.spotlight: Won good-ground 2m2f maiden at Downpatrick last July and some okay runs at same track after; pulled up on testing ground on first run of the year at Fairyhouse and has since left John McConnell.
runners.comment: Won good-ground 2m2f maiden at Downpatrick last July; pulled up on return; stable switcher

runners.name: Cool Summer
runners.spotlight: Some useful bumper form and was third in Roscommon maiden on hurdling debut; quiet since, including on first run of the year at Tramore in April, but has top rider on board for the first time on this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Third in Roscommon maiden; top rider on board for the first time on this handicap debut.

runners.name: Mollys Star
runners.spotlight: Keen-goer was sixth in a good bumper at Cork in March (winner and third won next time); fitted with a hood, still looked a tricky ride but ran much better on third hurdles start last month, keeping on when beaten 12l in decent maiden at Down Royal; handicap debut.
runners.comment: Fitted with a hood, still looked tricky ride but ran well when beaten 12l at Down Royal

runners.name: Patty O'Farrell
runners.spotlight: Having finished second at Naas in April, won this off 4lb lower last year and was third at Roscommon next time; hasn't threatened since on softer terrain but decent ground is key to her and she might bounce back to form; usual rider has jumped ship to Shannon Bank, but sub isn't too shabby.
runners.comment: Won this last year off 4lb lower; decent ground is key to her, might bounce back to form

runners.name: Chosen Shant
runners.spotlight: Long-standing maiden until joining yard last summer and then went back-to-back at Wexford in July/August; well beaten at the festival here on next start and has struggled since; better ground will help and she's 1lb lower than last winning mark.
runners.comment: Went back-to-back at Wexford last summer; has struggled since but better ground will help

runners.name: Mo Ghille Mar
runners.spotlight: Latest of three Flat wins for Jessica Harrington was off 84 last summer; improved of late over hurdles; mild promise when sixth at Bellewstown and then 22-1, dropped in grade to beat geldings at Wexford; needs proper good ground and trainer admits she's unpredictable.
runners.comment: Dropped in grade to beat geldings at Wexford; needs proper good ground and unpredictable

runners.name: Shannon Bank
runners.spotlight: Placed in two bumpers for Seamus Fahey and second in 2m1f Killarney handicap hurdle in October when with Gavin Cromwell; unfancied on stable debut, and first run of the year, when mid-field in Wexford chase; might prefer farther, but not out of it.
runners.comment: Unfancied on stable debut when mid-field in Wexford chase; might prefer farther

runners.name: Cinnabar Moth
runners.spotlight: Okay bumper run on debut but has been beaten miles in her maiden outings; handicap debut for well-related 5yo.
runners.comment: Has been beaten miles in her maiden outings; handicap debut for well-related 5yo

runners.name: Western Princess
runners.spotlight: Remote third at Wexford her best finishing position; can be keen and described as 'a very difficult ride' when connections were called in by the stewards after she was fifth at Ballinrobe; after a year's absence, ran in the corresponding race at Ballinrobe last week, keen again and had weakened out of it when falling at the last; readily opposed on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Had weakened out of it when falling at the last latest; opposable on handicap debut

runners.name: Inchiquin Star
runners.spotlight: Won three handicaps for previous yard and likes good ground; 1m6f Flat winner last year when her hurdles form wasn't great; has dropped down to last winning mark, but not a hectic record when fresh and last two wins have come in cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Not a hectic record when fresh and last two wins have come in cheekpieces

races.race_name: Join Racing TV With A Free Trial Now Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Putapoundinthejar
runners.spotlight: Failed to fire on the Flat last time but had previously returned to hurdles (dual winner) with a respectable fifth of 21 in a Listed handicap at Punchestown (2m, yielding); off the same mark here in a less demanding race.
runners.comment: Good fifth at Punchestown in April; off the same mark here in a less demanding race

runners.name: Wholelotofbusiness
runners.spotlight: Won a novice at Killarney in October (2m1f, yielding) and probably posted a similar effort when third in a handicap there last month; solid chance off an untouched mark.
runners.comment: Good third at Killarney last month and solid chance off an untouched mark

runners.name: Bossing It
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner who appeared to be going nowhere until a revived second of 14 at Wexford (2m1f, good to yielding); nudged up 2lb for that but a greater worry would be that this is a better race.
runners.comment: A revived second of 14 at Wexford but no question that this is a better race

runners.name: Becasse
runners.spotlight: French hurdle winner on very soft in October 2024; back from an absence to finish second in a Wexford handicap (soft) and departed too far out to tell what might have happened when 6-1 at Killarney; remains unexposed.
runners.comment: Lightly raced; second at Wexford before departing midrace when 6-1 at Killarney

runners.name: Spy
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose gelding who is consistent in the main and he was returning from a lengthy break when never that competitive at Punchestown a month ago; perhaps a shade high in the weights, even if he can return to best.
runners.comment: Quiet last time but after a break; consistent in the main; shade high in the weights

runners.name: Fast Felix
runners.spotlight: Lost his way in the winter but refreshed from a break he was a pleasing fourth at Killarney, finishing upsides shorter-priced stablemate Wholelotofbusiness; in the mix.
runners.comment: Lost his way in the winter but refreshed from a break was a pleasing fourth at Killarney

runners.name: Cappucino
runners.spotlight: Won his bumper here and added a Punchestown maiden hurdle; he's been good and bad in six defeats over fences and current wellbeing is a bit of an issue ahead of this return to hurdling.
runners.comment: Has been chasing; current wellbeing is a bit of an issue ahead of this return to hurdling

runners.name: Icare Desbois
runners.spotlight: Former Willie Mullins inmate; now looks regressive and was behind some of these at Killarney three weeks ago.
runners.comment: Now looks regressive and was behind some of these at Killarney three weeks ago

runners.name: Frankendael
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced since winning a Listed handicap here in September 2024; back from an absence, he was a much bigger price than Putapoundinthejar at Punchestown in April and duly finished well behind the Martin horse.
runners.comment: Course winner but well beaten at Punchestown on return from a long absence

races.race_name: John B. Keane Beginners Chase

runners.name: Whispering Willow
runners.spotlight: Much improved to make a successful stable debut in a 2m handicap hurdle at Punchestown and she nearly followed up on chase debut at Downpatrick (2m3f, good to yielding); jumped soundly enough that day and the one to beat here.
runners.comment: Won a Punchestown handicap hurdle before nearly following up on her chase debut

runners.name: Tropical Image
runners.spotlight: Her sole career win came in a 2m6f handicap hurdle here in September; beaten 22l in a 2m4f handicap on her belated chase debut but it wasn't a bad run.
runners.comment: Beaten 22l in a 2m4f handicap on her belated chase debut but it wans't a bad run

runners.name: Uptaurintop
runners.spotlight: Close up in a 2m handicap hurdle at Limerick (soft to heavy; first-time tongue-tie) before finishing well behind on his chase debut back there 11 days ago when an early mistake didn't help his cause; could have a part to play if avoiding similar mishaps.
runners.comment: Placed over hurdles before tailed off on chase debut when he made an early mistake

runners.name: Knockbrack Rambler
runners.spotlight: No closer than 20l in five defeats over fences and was pulled up back hurdling last time; testing ground was no excuse as he enjoys the mud.
runners.comment: No closer than 20l in five defeats over fences and was pulled up back hurdling last time

runners.name: Brave Endeavour
runners.spotlight: Point winner who went 0-6 over hurdles; led early but ended up tailed off in a 2m4f Limerick handicap on recent chase debut; he does have some ability and this is a desperately weak race.
runners.comment: Led early but ended up tailed off in a 2m4f Limerick handicap on recent chase debut

runners.name: Walk Away Harry
runners.spotlight: His best hurdles form for Charles Byrnes seems long gone and he's now regressive; well beaten in his previous chase this January.
runners.comment: His best hurdle form for Charles Byrnes seems long gone and he's now regressive

runners.name: Derby Rock
runners.spotlight: Failed to raise his modest profile when tailed off on chase debut at Tramore (2m6f, good); hard to warm to on this return from an absence.
runners.comment: Failed to raise his modest profile when tailed off on chase debut at Tramore (2m6f, good)

races.race_name: John J. Galvin Chase

runners.name: Jesse Evans
runners.spotlight: Five-time hurdle winner who is 1-9 over fences, though was runner-up in last summer's Galway Plate; all the sharper for his comeback run when beaten 4l in a 2m4f Grade 3 at Killarney three weeks ago and he's a player on these terms.
runners.comment: Beaten 4l in a 2m4f Grade 3 at Killarney three weeks ago and he's a player on these terms

runners.name: Blood Destiny
runners.spotlight: Versatile ground-wise; won this last year and has since added a fourth chase win in a Grade 3 at Naas; ran second to Energumene in a Grade 2 in April before his no-show in a Grade 1 last time; has clear claims back at this level, even having to concede weight all round.
runners.comment: Has clear claims back at this level, even having to concede weight all round

runners.name: Classic Getaway
runners.spotlight: Hasn't been at his best since winning last year's Grade 2 Red Mills at Gowran but a couple of subsequent runs read well, especially in the context of today's easier assignment; unlikely to be far away; any rain welcomed.
runners.comment: Not been at his best for a while but this is easier than he's accustomed to

runners.name: Eagle Fang
runners.spotlight: Excellent efforts in deep 3m handicap hurdles in recent weeks, with the new visor working well; still early days over fences and the form he showed in winning at Thurles suggests he could be as effective in this discipline.
runners.comment: Excellent efforts in deep 3m handicap hurdles in recent weeks; has won over fences

runners.name: Lisleigh Lad
runners.spotlight: Dual hunter chase winner who can hold his own in handicaps but unlikely to be overly competitive at this level.
runners.comment: Can hold his own in handicaps but unlikely to be overly competitive at this level

runners.name: Itsalladream
runners.spotlight: Has won a maiden hurdle and a novice chase; has looked a bit outclassed in Grade 3s in his last three races and a similar scenario seems likely in this company (well behind Jesse Evans last time).
runners.comment: Has been struggling in Grade 3s and similar scenario seems likely in this company

races.race_name: Beasley Engineering Hunters Chase

runners.name: Ambitious Fellow
runners.spotlight: Four wins over hurdles (rated 130), one of them here; maiden after 10 chase starts and has had jumping issues, but was second off 120 at Roscommon last summer; only beaten 7l in Champion Hunters Chase at Punchestown where he was a huge price.
runners.comment: Only beaten 7l in Champion Hunters Chase at Punchestown where he was a huge price

runners.name: What Path
runners.spotlight: Useful 3m hurdler for Willie Mullins before fruitless spell over fences in Britain; seems to have been revitalised by point-to-points, winning three in the spring; in contention when slipping up in banks race at Punchestown; chased home Solitary Man at Killarney and 3lb better off here.
runners.comment: Revitalised by point-to-points; chased home Solitary Man at Killarney and 3lb better off

runners.name: Solitary Man
runners.spotlight: Has a particular fondness for Killarney and won for the fifth time there three weeks ago, where he beat What Path by 1.5l over a slightly shorter trip; was just doing enough, so headgear back on; likes good ground.
runners.comment: Was just doing enough when he beat What Path at Killarney, headgear back on
runners.owner: Shanahan/Mrs M V Magnier/Mrs Carthy/Mrs Bartlett

runners.name: Bartlemy Boy
runners.spotlight: Good record in point-to-points, winning five; having won his first hunter chase start at Killarney, was favourite when second in this last year; well held in a couple of hunter chases this year and departed early on at Killarney.
runners.comment: Favourite when second in this last year; well held in a couple of hunter chases this year

runners.name: Asphalt Cowboy
runners.spotlight: All three hunter chase wins have come at Downpatrick, including a repeat of last year's victory in 3m5f contest just 10 days ago; trainer admits he's a different horse at that track, which renders him opposable here.
runners.comment: All three hunter chase wins have come at Downpatrick and opposable here

runners.name: Heartbreak Weather
runners.spotlight: Couple of falls either side of point win in March; keeping on in second on rules debut when exiting two out in 2m4f hunter chase at Punchestown.
runners.comment: Keeping on in second when exiting two out in 2m4f hunter chase at Punchestown

runners.name: Hunt Away
runners.spotlight: Useful point-to-point form including three wins; narrowly beaten in 3m maiden hunter chase at Cork, but this is way tougher.
runners.comment: Narrowly beaten in 3m maiden hunter chase at Cork, but this is way tougher.

runners.name: Five Star Getaway
runners.spotlight: Old boy was smart handicapper in Britain back in the day; took advantage of his rivals' mishaps when winning at Fairyhouse; beaten a long way in the Champion Hunter Chase at Punchestown.
runners.comment: Took advantage of his rivals' mishaps when winning at Fairyhouse; beaten a long way latest

runners.name: In Excelsis Deo
runners.spotlight: Won a Grade 2 handicap over 2m4f a couple of years ago for Harry Fry before losing form; some good efforts in point-to-points, including recent win under this jockey; previously, was only around 7l off stablemate Solitary Man over 2m4f at Cork (disqualified, rider weighed in marginally light).
runners.comment: Only around 7l off stablemate Solitary Man over 2m4f at Cork and won point since

runners.name: Big Interest
runners.spotlight: Looked a smart prospect when beating Its On The Line at Down Royal in December 2024; disappointing overall since then, except for an 18l Fairyhouse win last year; 66-1 at Punchestown festival, still prominent when exiting a mile out; well held in a point a week later.
runners.comment: 66-1 at Punchestown festival, exiteda mile out; well held in a point a week later.

runners.name: Call The Tune
runners.spotlight: Useful soft-ground staying handicapper for Barry Connell back in the day; yet to win a point but only 2l off What Path in banks' contest in February; well held over the banks at Punchestown last month.
runners.comment: Second to What Path in banks' point in February; well held over the banks at Punchestown

races.race_name: Bryan MacMahon (Pro/Am) INH Flat Race

runners.name: Minnatrix
runners.spotlight: Ran to a useful level when runner-up in a Roscommon bumper (good to yielding); surprisingly lengthy odds at 80-1 for his recent hurdle debut at Killarney where he was on the retreat when falling (in first-time hood); on that debut effort he commands respect.
runners.comment: Likeable bumper debut; 80-1 and beaten when falling on his hurdle debut

runners.name: Thelittleballerina
runners.spotlight: Built on her pleasing debut to win on yielding ground at Clonmel (2m4f) by a comfortable 8l; didn't beat rubbish that day and, from the family of Bellshill, she looks a nice prospect.
runners.comment: Clonmel winner and, from the family of Bellshill, she looks a nice prospect

runners.name: Martymill
runners.spotlight: Not without hope on the strength of his debut run when fourth at Fairyhouse but unable to match that form since; however, getting back on softer ground could help (should it rain) and so too might cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Respected on the strength of his debut effort and rain would be welcomed

runners.name: Echoes Of Jane
runners.spotlight: Signs of ability in both her point runs and her yard has won the last two renewals of this bumper; hood added.
runners.comment: Signs of ability in both her point runs and her yard has won the last two renewals of this

runners.name: Cloonbonniffe
runners.spotlight: Beaten about 11l at Sligo where he weakened when it mattered; has ability but needs more here.
runners.comment: Beaten about 11l at Sligo where he weakened when it mattered; has ability but needs more

runners.name: Gusserane
runners.spotlight: Makes no appeal after pulling up in his two points.
runners.comment: Makes no appeal after pulling up in his two points

runners.name: An Bhoireann
runners.spotlight: Beaten 82l and 25l in his two bumpers; now has tongue tied; couldn't recommend.
runners.comment: Beaten 82l and 25l in his two bumpers; now has tongue tied; couldn't recommend

runners.name: Caman Hill
runners.spotlight: Drew clear for a 9l success at Ballinrobe (good) last May and that form has worked out well, with both placed horses progressing to better things; the absence is worrying but he's clearly useful.
runners.comment: Drew clear for 9l success at Ballinrobe (good) last May and that form has worked out well

runners.name: Whatdoyoucallhim
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in three bumpers and suffered the same fate on hurdle debut; cheekpieces tried.
runners.comment: Tailed off in three bumpers and suffered the same fate on hurdle debut; cheekpieces tried

runners.name: The Boysofairhill
runners.spotlight: Took five goes to win a point and only fifth of eight in last month's bumper, though he did race freely; others preferred.
runners.comment: Took five goes to win a point and only fifth of eight in last month's bumper

runners.name: Rusty Finure
runners.spotlight: Beaten a shade under 60l in bumpers here and at Limerick; the last was in late 2024.
runners.comment: Beaten a shade under 60l in bumpers here and at Limerick; the last was in late 2024

races.race_name: Get 100/1 On England At BetVictor EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Bai Tong
runners.spotlight: Beaten odds-on favourite in both runs this spring, showing better form when second at Ripon (6f, good) two weeks ago; likely she can be found a similar race but perhaps one with fewer notable newcomers than this one.
runners.comment: Beaten at odds-on in both starts; can win a maiden but perhaps not this one

runners.name: Scommessa Sicura
runners.spotlight: Her inexperience was evident on her Yarmouth debut (6f, good to soft), drifting to the far rail over 2f out but keeping on for second behind a cosy winner; open to improvement; one to take seriously.
runners.comment: Inexperience evident but still shaped with promise when 2nd at Yarmouth last month; chance

runners.name: Aperoll
runners.spotlight: 80,000gns yearling; fifth foal; Ghaiyyath half-sister to US 5.5f/1m turf winner Alexis Zorba; dam unplaced 5f-7f 2yo (RPR 38), sister to 6f Group 2 winner Royal Intervention; powerful stable; one to consider.
runners.comment: 80,000gns half-sister to a US turf winner out of a well-connected maiden; one to consider

runners.name: Enhancing
runners.spotlight: 70,000gns yearling by Too Darn Hot; sixth foal; half-sister to multiple 6f winner Abbey Heights (RPR 92); dam a maiden half-sister to US 1m1f Grade 1 winner Singhalese; stable's 2yos going well and she's a newcomer of note.
runners.comment: 70,000gns half-sister to multiple 6f winner Abbey Heights; yard's 2yos going well

runners.name: Avalon Queen
runners.spotlight: 50,000gns yearling; by Masar; fourth foal of a French 1m2f-1m4f winner (including Group 2; RPR 111), herself a half-sister to 1m4f Listed winner Emotion, out of French 2m4f Group 1 winner Molly Malone; should have a future but more of a long-term prospect than some.
runners.comment: 50,000gns yearling; dam a Group 2 winner over middle distances; longer-term prospect

runners.name: Desert Sands
runners.spotlight: £40,000 breeze-up 2yo; third foal; half-sister to 6f 2yo/1m winner Callisto Dream (RPR 83); dam a 7f/7.5f winner (98); yard has had a winning 2yo newcomer this year; worth a market check.
runners.comment: £40,000 breeze-up 2yo; half-sister to a 6f-1m winner out of useful 7f winner; check market

runners.name: Please Don't Go
runners.spotlight: 85,000euros yearling by Ghayyaith; eighth foal; half-sister to four winners including Fortunino (Italian 1m3f-1m7f including Listed; RPR 105), Fresnel (1m AW; 99) and Sea Lantern (1m4f; 87); unraced dam closely related to 1m4f-1m6f Group 3 winner Profound Beauty; stable has a healthy strike-rate with 2yos this year; one to note for market confidence.
runners.comment: 85,000euros half-sister to 4 winners; unraced dam from good family; yard's 2yos going well

runners.name: Eabha
runners.spotlight: By Space Blues; first foal of an unraced half-sister to winning hurdler Ebasari out of a useful mare from the family of Gold Cup winners Estimate and Enzeli; others may have her measure today.
runners.comment: Dam from a strong staying family; others may have her measure today

runners.name: Attack Attack
runners.spotlight: By Palace Pier; first foal of a 7f 2yo winner (including Group 3; RPR 104), herself out of a Listed-winning sprinter; stable going great guns, including with 2yos, and she's a strong contender on paper.
runners.comment: Dam a 7f Group 3 winner as a 2yo; yard going well, including with juveniles; good claims

runners.name: Goblet
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; half-sister to 6f winner Dance And Romance (RPR 89); dam 1m and 1m2f (Listed) winner (106), half-sister to 5f Group 3 winner Cotai Glory; winning 2yo newcomers for the stable are rare, but not unheard of.
runners.comment: Half-sister to a useful 6f winner out of a 1m/1m2f Listed winner; yard's 2yo record modest
runners.owner: J F Dean, Mrs J Scott & Lady Trenchard

runners.name: Tall Trees
runners.spotlight: £42,000 yearling; ninth foal; by Showcasing; closely related to three winners including Sash (1m including AW/USA; RPR 99) and Onight (7f AW/UAE; 96), half-sister to two winners including White Willow (9.4f-1m4f; 89); dam 1m 2yo winner (80), out of 1m2f Group 1 winner; stable 0-6 with 2yos in 2026.
runners.comment: £42,000 yearling; five winning siblings; dam from good family; yard 0-6 with 2yos in 2026

runners.name: Wintour
runners.spotlight: 160,000euros yearling; by Supremacy; tenth foal; sister to 7.5f winner Substance (RPR 81), half-sister to winners Ejtilaab (6f/7f including AW; 116), Eqtiraan (6f-1m1f including 2yo/Listed; 114) and Licence To Conquer (7f; 73); dam unraced half-sister to 5f Group 3 winner Majestic Missile; lots to like on paper.
runners.comment: 160,000euros yearling; half-sister to two useful winners; likely type on paper

races.race_name: BetVictor Backing England To Win Handicap

runners.name: River King
runners.spotlight: Off the mark upped to 1m2f at Windsor last June; gelded prior to finishing second on his reappearance/handicap debut 11 days ago (1m2f, good to soft), pulling clear with the winner having travelled strongly; a 3lb rise won't help but he isn't fully exposed and returning to 1m shouldn't be an issue.
runners.comment: Heavily backed on reappearance 11 days ago but could only manage 2nd; 3lb rise not ideal

runners.name: North View
runners.spotlight: Produced a strong finish to win a competitive 7f handicap here as a 3yo; first win since when making a successful stable debut at Chelmsford in January (7f); in and out since but he's on a winning mark and the ability is there if things pan out favourably.
runners.comment: Course winner; on winning mark; mixed bag this year but he's capable if things fall right

runners.name: Noodle Mission
runners.spotlight: May have needed the run at Kempton 12 days ago but he ended up well beaten; he has a lower turf mark to exploit but is a better horse on AW; others look safer.
runners.comment: Better on AW but handicapped accordingly; needs to step up on recent reappearance

runners.name: Freddie's Star
runners.spotlight: Down the field in both handicaps after winning a Chelmsford novice at odds of 1-16f; hopes rest on the step up in trip having a positive effect.
runners.comment: Well beaten in two handicaps after his maiden success; new trip needs to turn things round

runners.name: The Third Star
runners.spotlight: 7f maiden win on AW last April; faced some stiff tasks afterwards and came up short; well backed on last month's seasonal/stable debut (7f, AW), staying on best (fastest final-furlong sectional) for fifth in a race not run to suit; worth another crack at 1m on that evidence; may yet do better.
runners.comment: Promising stable debut at Lingfield (7f, AW); worth another crack at a mile; not ruled out

runners.name: Herculeus
runners.spotlight: C&D winner last August (good ground; cheekpieces first time); three heavy defeats have followed and he comes here with plenty to prove.
runners.comment: C&D winner last August but three heavy defeats have followed; plenty to prove

runners.name: Jakarta
runners.spotlight: Dual winning juvenile (maiden/nursery) who was off for 324 days before showing mixed form in three starts from September last year, just failing in Newmarket handicap (1m, good) but lesser shows either side of that; she missed two runs on account of the ground (good to firm) last month; tongue-tie now added; looks on a handy mark.
runners.comment: Returns from 228 days off with a tongue-tie fitted; still low mileage; could do with rain

runners.name: Lunario
runners.spotlight: Won twice over shorter last summer; big prices and little to cheer this year; down in the weights but he has plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Should be well treated but 2026 efforts leave plenty to be desired

runners.name: Electrifarhh
runners.spotlight: Only seen twice since winning a 1m fillies' novice as a 2yo (Polytrack), facing a stiff task on the first occasion and then not seen to best effect over 7f nine weeks ago; not fully exposed.
runners.comment: Not seen much racing since winning a 2yo novice; remains capable of better

runners.name: Knights Gold
runners.spotlight: A 2025 campaign that featured five wins was always going to be a hard act to follow and he has found it tough going so far this year; has slipped back to a winning mark though and cheekpieces are now given a go.
runners.comment: Fine 2025 campaign saw him win five times; struggled this year but cheekpieces now tried

runners.name: Green Triangle
runners.spotlight: Won twice in Ireland for Joseph O'Brien; retains ability but the handicapper looks to be holding the aces at present; drops in class today but may need some more weight off his back.
runners.comment: Handicapper on top since joining current stable; drops in class but needs to pull out more

runners.name: Classic Encounter
runners.spotlight: Took full advantage of the drop into Class 4 company when readily seeing off five rivals at Redcar last week (1m, good to firm); penalised in a deeper race but he still commands serious respect.
runners.comment: Readily saw off 5 rivals at Redcar last week (1m, good to firm); key player under penalty

races.race_name: Favourite Things Boosted Daily At BetVictor Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Jellystone Park
runners.spotlight: Promise at Lingfield in March (6f, AW) and duly stepped forward when a front-running second of ten at Doncaster last month (6f, good; beaten a nose); now tongue tied; leading form claims.
runners.comment: Good front-running second at Doncaster last month; now tongue tied; bold bid expected

runners.name: Sea Cookie
runners.spotlight: Second over C&D on last July's debut and he filled the same spot at Wolverhampton one month later; gelded and had a wind op since last seen; should be in the thick of it.
runners.comment: Second in both 2yo runs last summer, the first over C&D; gelded/wind op since latest

runners.name: Miss Nightcap
runners.spotlight: She was an unconsidered 40-1 shot prior to her Windsor debut last month (6f, good to firm) but she ran well for second, unable to live with the winner, who has since won a handicap in good style off a BHA mark of 85, in the final furlong; can do better and shouldn't be underestimated.
runners.comment: Shaped nicely in 2nd behind a useful winner on last month's Windsor debut; can do better
runners.owner: E S-O Racing Club And Elaine Chivers

runners.name: Majestic Ruler
runners.spotlight: 180,000euros foal, 220,000gns yearling; third foal; half-brother to 7f/1m4f winner Caught U Looking (including 2yo Group 3; RPR 104); dam unraced half-sister to winners Mix And Mingle (7f Group 3) and Double Or Bubble (6f/7f Group 3); tongue tied for debut; looks the part on paper.
runners.comment: 220,000gns yearling; half-brother to a Group 3 winner; starts out at a realistic level
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Bold Shout
runners.spotlight: £52,000 yearling; eighth foal; half-brother to winners Rainbow Royal (6f-1m 2yo including Italian Group 3; RPR 102), Rose Mamara (5f-7f; 83), Nibras Rainbow (7f-10.6f; 69) and Yellow Dream (French 10.4f AW); dam 6f winner (including Listed; 96); starts out at a realistic level; betting to guide.
runners.comment: £52,000 half-brother to four winners; starts out at a realistic level; check the market

runners.name: Velvet Goldmine
runners.spotlight: 28-1 when a well-beaten eighth of 11 in a Newmarket fillies' maiden (7f, good) last October; bred to do better at some point.
runners.comment: 28-1 and well beaten in a Newmarket fillies' maiden last October; bred to do better

runners.name: Fozzy Osbourne
runners.spotlight: 300-1 when a never-dangerous ninth of 12 at Salisbury nine days ago (6f, good); no appeal.
runners.comment: 300-1 when a never-dangerous ninth of 12 at Salisbury nine days ago (6f, good); no appeal

races.race_name: Pump Technology Jonathan And Natalie Handicap

runners.name: Unico
runners.spotlight: Back to a winning mark and last month's return to action saw her caught out wide at Kempton; drops to 5f for the first time on her 28th start; others appeal more.
runners.comment: On a winning mark but below par on her last two runs and drops to 5f for the first time

runners.name: Spring Bloom
runners.spotlight: His losing run is gathering momentum and he has not won over 5f for three years; went close at Goodwood last month (5f, good) though and he should remain a potent force at this level.
runners.comment: Capable at this level but it is over three years since his last 5f win; each-way shout

runners.name: Merrimack
runners.spotlight: Better strike-rate on turf than AW; solid third at Windsor three weeks ago (for Chloe Lyons) and has a chance if in the same form; finished behind a couple of today's rivals in a C&D handicap last summer.
runners.comment: Solid third at Windsor three weeks ago; held by two of these on a C&D run last summer

runners.name: The Feminine Urge
runners.spotlight: Ran well for third on her stable debut at Bath in April (5f, good); fair effort three weeks later but she took a backwards step 20 days ago; edging down the weights but others look safer.
runners.comment: Not kicked on from her promising stable debut in two runs since; others appeal more

runners.name: Hint Of Humour
runners.spotlight: First past the post four times in succession in the second half of 2025 (demoted to fourth on the third occasion); she has carried on the good work this year, finishing second in three of her four starts; more required to defy this mark and she still has a bit to prove on turf.
runners.comment: Made good progress on AW since last seen on turf; needs a bit more to defy this mark

runners.name: Harry Brown
runners.spotlight: Took advantage of a career-low mark to land his first win on turf at Lingfield (good to firm) three weeks ago; up 4lb but he has the ability to deal with it.
runners.comment: Came from off the pace to win at Lingfield (5f, good to firm) last time; chance up 4lb
runners.owner: Peter Crate And Gallagher Bloodstock Ltd

runners.name: Faustus
runners.spotlight: Three C&D runs have yielded two wins and a fifth (in Class 3 company); has only run to form once this year, finishing third to Harry Brown at Lingfield (5f, good to firm) last month; below par last time but if he is to revive it is likely to be under these conditions.
runners.comment: 2-3 over C&D; mixed in 2026 but if he's to revive it's likely to be under these conditions

runners.name: Aces Wild
runners.spotlight: Ran twice over C&D last summer and performed with great credit on both occasions, finishing second to Faustus in July and filling the same position one month later; career-best effort when winning at Bath last week (5f, firm); penalty demands more but he can't be discounted.
runners.comment: Two good C&D runs last summer and he arrives on the back of a Bath win; should go well

races.race_name: South Downs Water Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Personal Best
runners.spotlight: Consistent since handicapping including a win at Ffos Las (1m6f, soft) just under a year ago; may have needed last month's Salisbury return (1m6f, good), but good to firm ground would be a concern.
runners.comment: May have needed last month's Salisbury return but good to firm ground would be a concern
runners.owner: Hot To Trot 1 & Mrs R Pease

runners.name: Naval Tribute
runners.spotlight: Has done well since switched to Flat turf for his last two starts with the visor added, winning at Beverley (1m4f, good to firm) in April before second of 12 in an apprentice handicap at Ascot (2m, good) last month (winner followed up at Chester on Saturday); up just another 1lb and high on the list.
runners.comment: Has done well since switched to Flat turf for his last two starts; high on the list

runners.name: Calvert
