runners.spotlight: 1-20, but no doubting his consistency and was only just behind Naval Tribute when third of 12 in an apprentice handicap at Ascot (2m, good) last month; has had more chances than his old rival, but should again go well from the same mark.
runners.comment: Only just behind Naval Tribute when third at Ascot last month; should go well again

runners.name: John Galt
runners.spotlight: Showed a fine attitude when just getting the better of a subsequent winner to make it seventh-time lucky on turf debut at Leicester (1m4f, good to firm) last month; 3lb higher, but remains unexposed and is shortlisted if seeing out the longer trip (stays 1m6f).
runners.comment: 3lb higher than when narrowly off the mark last time; shortlisted if staying longer trip

runners.name: Now The Eagle
runners.spotlight: Ex-French; no questioning his stamina with the form of his last three starts at Kempton (2m, AW) reading 122; has something to prove under these conditions, though.
runners.comment: Form of last three starts at Kempton reads 122; something to prove under these conditions

runners.name: Vaguely Royal
runners.spotlight: Third in his last three starts, but may not have achieved that much and remains without a win on the Flat since the end of 2023; others more compelling.
runners.comment: Remains without a win on the Flat since the end of 2023; others more compelling

runners.name: Baileys Khelstar
runners.spotlight: Missed the whole of 2025; needs to put a lesser effort in a 0-95 contest at Hamilton (1m5f, good) last month behind him, but this is easier and he is just 1lb higher than for his last win, which came over this trip.
runners.comment: 1lb higher than for his last win, which came over this trip; this is easier than last time
runners.owner: Baileys Horse Feeds (F H Nash Ltd)

runners.name: Barenboim
runners.spotlight: Just 1lb above his last winning mark, but is 6-31 on the AW and 0-18 on turf, including six starts over hurdles; others more solid.
runners.comment: 6-31 on the AW and 0-18 on turf, including six starts over hurdles; others more solid

races.race_name: West Court Retreat Supports Heros Charity Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Twisting Physics
runners.spotlight: Won two of his first four starts, including at this track on his handicap debut last June (7f, good); both runs this season have been respectable and he could take a step forward with a tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: Two creditable runs this year; tongue-tie added today; could still have more to offer

runners.name: Ted Le Saux
runners.spotlight: Down the field on his handicap debut at Haydock in April (extended 1m2f, good to firm); in good hands but doesn't appeal as being ahead of his mark.
runners.comment: Down the field on his handicap debut in April; mark looks high enough

runners.name: Bintabuha
runners.spotlight: Won a 1m2f handicap at Wetherby last April but found life tougher afterwards (highly tried); left William Knight prior to her Yarmouth reappearance, a race where she shaped as though the run would bring her on; better expected this time.
runners.comment: Promising 5th on seasonal/stable debut; capable of better & she's an interesting contender

runners.name: Nanino Niyati
runners.spotlight: 1m2f winner for the Kublers last season; not beaten far into fourth in an Ascot handicap (1m2f, good to firm) on her stable debut last month; after just six runs she may do better but such progress is essential.
runners.comment: Solid 4th on seasonal/stable debut at Ascot last month; others perhaps better treated

runners.name: Victors Spirit
runners.spotlight: Fell in both hurdle starts, after which he was rerouted to the Flat; made a successful handicap debut at Southwell (1m3f, hooded first time) in April and two runs since have both been respectable; more needed to come out on top in this field.
runners.comment: On the up since switched to handicaps but this might prove too competitive
runners.owner: The Ridgeway Racing For Fun Partnership

runners.name: Princess Rascal
runners.spotlight: She made a low-key return to action at Salisbury four weeks ago but she had been on the up as a 3yo and she could continue that progress with a run under her belt.
runners.comment: Low-key return to action last month but progressive in 2025 and she should be sharper now

runners.name: Morcar
runners.spotlight: 1lb lower than for his last win; two runs back from wind surgery haven't been his best and he is taking on some potential improvers today.
runners.comment: On a fair mark and conditions will suit but not shone yet this year; takes on improvers

runners.name: Wanderlust
runners.spotlight: Fair form in France at up to 1m4f; 50-1 for his stable debut at Chester (10.3f, good) this month and he ran well for a close fourth; drops into a Class 4 and he could yet do better; good to firm ground would be a first.
runners.comment: Promise in France and again on stable debut last month; good to firm would be a first

runners.name: Aphrodite Spirit
runners.spotlight: Improved with each of her three runs as a 3yo, getting off the mark when upped to 1m2f at Lingfield (AW) last September; unexposed but she is conceding race fitness to her rivals and this may not be her day.
runners.comment: Unexposed but she is conceding race fitness to her rivals; this may not be her day

races.race_name: Fenchurch Insurance Brokers Handicap

runners.name: Solar Edge
runners.spotlight: Made light of a five-month absence when making all in 5f handicap at Bath 35 days ago, holding on by a nose from The Thames Boatman; not taken lightly despite a 3lb rise.
runners.comment: Made light of five-month absence when scoring at Bath; not taken lightly despite 3lb rise

runners.name: Cressida Wildes
runners.spotlight: Scored at Wolverhampton (5f) in February, but she was off for ten weeks before beating only one in 5f handicap at Ascot 23 days ago; has a bit to prove now.
runners.comment: Off ten weeks before beating one in h'cap at Ascot 23 days ago; has a bit to prove now

runners.name: Wheels Of Fire
runners.spotlight: Resumed winning ways at Musselburgh (5f) in April but below form all three runs since, fourth of six at Haydock (5f) 11 days ago; this course winner is handily weighted if back on his A-game though.
runners.comment: Resumed winning ways at Musselburgh (5f) in April but below form all three runs since

runners.name: HK Fourteen
runners.spotlight: A three-time winner (including over C&D) in 2025; off six months before a fading tenth in 6f Doncaster handicap on his recent return and he should strip sharper here.
runners.comment: C&D winner; off six months before fading Doncaster tenth on return; should strip sharper

runners.name: Diomed Duke
runners.spotlight: Scored at Lingfield (5f, AW) last June and signed off with a good third of ten in 5f Yarmouth handicap in October; in the mix if fully tuned up for his seasonal return.
runners.comment: Signed off for 2025 with good Yarmouth third in October; in the mix if fully tuned up

runners.name: The Thames Boatman
runners.spotlight: Multiple AW winner; quirky sort who often starts slowly but he finally got off the mark (in a change of headgear) on turf in 5f Goodwood handicap last month; up 3lb but he can make his presence felt again.
runners.comment: Finally off the mark on turf at Goodwood last month; up 3lb but can make his presence felt

runners.name: Mesaafi
runners.spotlight: On a losing run but he got right back on track with fourth of 17 in handicap at Ascot (5f) last month; can go well off an unchanged mark.
runners.comment: On a losing run but right back on track with fourth of 17 at Ascot last month; can go well

runners.name: Moulin Booj
runners.spotlight: C&D scorer; not seen to best effect on his last three runs since scoring at Wolverhampton (5f) in March, going too freely from an outside draw when last of six at Bath (5f) last time; interesting from an easing mark.
runners.comment: C&D scorer; too free from wide draw when last at Bath latest; interesting from easing mark

races.race_name: Pourou Garhigh Anniversary EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (EBF Qual') (GBB Race) (Div I)

runners.name: Envision
runners.spotlight: 5-1 and green, made an encouraging start when a keeping-on fourth of ten in maiden at Thirsk (5f, good) 16 days ago; this Mohaather filly can build on it over this longer trip; blinkers added.
runners.comment: Encouraging start with fourth at Thirsk; can build on it up to 6f with blinkers added

runners.name: Emirates Express
runners.spotlight: Earthlight gelding; half-brother to four winners, notably Goldinthesea (1m; RPR 96); dam Listed-placed 7.4f winner (RPR 97); a noteworthy newcomer, especially if the market vibes are positive.
runners.comment: Earthlight gelding; half-brother to four winners; a noteworthy newcomer

runners.name: Lola
runners.spotlight: 7,000gns foal, 42,000euros yearling; half-sister to three winners, notably King Cuan (Group-placed 6f/7f; RPR 112); dam, 7f winner (RPR 85); this Caturra filly appeals on paper so enters calculations.
runners.comment: Appeals on paper so this Caturra filly enters calculations

runners.name: Bossy Angel
runners.spotlight: £11,000 yearling; Pinatubo filly; dam unraced half-sister to smart winners Asjad (6f/6.5f) and Blue Storm (5f); the betting can prove an accurate guide for this newcomer.
runners.comment: Pinatubo filly; the betting can prove an accurate guide for this newcomer

runners.name: Chianti
runners.spotlight: 20,000gns yearling; dam, unplaced over 6f-8.6f (RPR 59), is a half-sister to 1m1f/1m3f Flat and 2m2f/2m3f hurdle winner Bruant; yard is going well with its juveniles so this Tasleet filly rates an interesting newcomer.
runners.comment: Yard going well so this Tasleet filly rates an interesting newcomer

runners.name: My Boo Boo
runners.spotlight: Ten Sovereigns colt; half-brother to 1m/1m1f winner Salam Dubawi (RPR 93); dam unraced sister to 1m2f/11.4f winner Placidia; market can guide.
runners.comment: Ten Sovereigns colt; the market can guide on his debut

runners.name: Lion Of Persia
runners.spotlight: 40,000euros yearling; dam unraced half-sister to winners Spice Route (US 8.5f Grade 2) and Zurbriggen (useful 7f) out of an Italian Oaks winner; this son of Persian Force can't be ruled out on his first run.
runners.comment: This son of Persian Force can't be ruled out on his first run

runners.name: Minnie Bear
runners.spotlight: St Mark's Basilica filly; well held (over 5f) in Wolverhampton maiden and Yarmouth novice this spring; needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: St Mark's Basilica filly; well held in Wolverhampton maiden and Yarmouth novice

runners.name: Ventura Power
runners.spotlight: 16-1, hinted at promise when sixth of 12 in maiden here (6f, good to firm) on his debut nine days ago; this son of Perfect Power can build on it for good yard.
runners.comment: Hinted at promise with debut sixth here; can build on it for good yard

runners.name: Emerald Bay
runners.spotlight: Son of Bayside Boy who has offered little in a pair of 6f Wolverhampton novices this spring; lots more is needed.
runners.comment: Son of Bayside Boy; has offered little in a pair of 6f Wolverhampton novices this spring

races.race_name: Pourou Garhigh Anniversary EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (EBF Qual') (GBB Race) (Div II)

runners.name: Dowman
runners.spotlight: Sioux Nation colt who has shaped with promise this spring when runner-up in 5f novice at Bath and then 5.5f maiden at the same course, weakening late on latter occasion; big shout back at 5f with tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: Twice runner-up at Bath, on latter occasion weakening late over 5.5f; big shout back at 5f

runners.name: Belardo Ace
runners.spotlight: 16-1, hinted at promise with a debut fifth of nine in novice at Salisbury (5f, good) nine days ago; this son of Belardo is in very good hands and can take a step forward.
runners.comment: Hinted at promise with debut fifth at Salisbury; in good hands and can take step forward

runners.name: Space Dreamer
runners.spotlight: £9,000 yearling; Space Traveller filly; half-sister to Czech 5f-7f winner Rabbit Zoustar; dam, placed over 6f (RPR 78), is a half-sister to Canadian 1m4f Grade 1 winner Wigmore Hall and US 1m1f Grade 1 winner Ocean Road; possibilities on her debut.
runners.comment: Space Traveller filly; possibilities on her debut

runners.name: Alpaca's Bullet
runners.spotlight: Daughter of Soldier's Call still looked green when a never-dangerous seventh of ten in 5f Yarmouth novice 12 days ago; can do better in due course.
runners.comment: Still looked green when seventh at Yarmouth 12 days ago; can do better in due course

runners.name: Good Fighter
runners.spotlight: £50,000 yearling, £22,000 2yo; half-brother to winners Jumbeau (Listed-placed 5f; RPR 94) and Sands Of Dubai (7f; RPR 60); dam, 7f AW winner (RPR 76), is out of US 1m winning sister to 1m/1m1f Grade 2 winner Justwhistledixie; this son of A'Ali needs considering, especially if the market vibes are positive.
runners.comment: This son of A'Ali needs considering, especially if the market vibes are positive

runners.name: Sovereign Dawn
runners.spotlight: 26,000euros yearling, 3,000gns 2yo; Coulsty colt; half-brother to 5f winner End Of Story (RPR 90); dam, unraced half-sister to winners Inverleigh (5.7f Listed) and Setarhe (Group-placed 6f 2yo), is out of an unraced half-sister to German 1m2f Group 1 winner Ransom O'War; the betting should prove an accurate guide for this newcomer.
runners.comment: Coulsty colt; the betting should prove an accurate guide for this newcomer

runners.name: Arcadian Days
runners.spotlight: Lope Y Fernandez filly; well held in a pair of 5f novices at Redcar and Thirsk this spring; significantly more is required.
runners.comment: Well held at Redcar and Thirsk this spring; significantly more is required

runners.name: Uncle Frank
runners.spotlight: 1,000gns yearling, £20,000 2yo; dam, 5f winner (RPR 78), is a half-sister to 9.4f/1m2f winner Shaheen Saqaar, out of German 7f Listed winner; stable's juveniles are going well so this Ardad colt is a likely player starting out here.
runners.comment: Stable's juveniles are going well so this Ardad colt is a likely player starting out here

runners.name: Our Fella
runners.spotlight: 66-1, well-held fifth of seven in novice at Bath (5f, good) on his debut 20 days ago; lots more is required from this son of Soldier's Call.
runners.comment: Well-held fifth of seven at Bath on his debut; lots more is required

runners.name: Fly Test
runners.spotlight: 40-1, well-held 11th of 13 in novice at Newmarket (6f, good to firm) on his debut 17 days ago; this son of Bungle Inthejungle should have learned from the experience.
runners.comment: Well-held 11th at Newmarket on his debut; should have learned from the experience

races.race_name: Ravens Roofing Supplies Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Gold Digger
runners.spotlight: Both runs over 6f on good to firm ground, making a successful debut at Yarmouth (9-4) in May last year (form worked out well) and finishing sixth of 17 in the Albany at Royal Ascot (7-1); the latter was a creditable performance given a slow break and then taking a good hold; this looks a good opportunity on her return, even under her penalty.
runners.comment: Yarmouth winner and sixth in the Albany last year; this looks a good opportunity on return

runners.name: Red Legs
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam twice-raced half-sister to winners Annie Salts (5f), Flashy Diva (6f 2yo) and Mister Mackenzie (German/French/Belgian 6.5f-8.5f); probably best watched on debut; stable also runs Rianka.
runners.comment: First foal; probably best watched on debut

runners.name: March Of Time
runners.spotlight: 70,000euros foal; closely related to 1m2f turf/1m4f AW winner Time To Blossom (RPR 89), half-sister to three winners including Repeater (7f-1m4f including 2yo; 112) and All Out (Group-placed 5f/6f 2yo; 102); dam 1m winner (74); bred to appreciate further in time but still respected on debut in an uncompetitive race, especially if backed.
runners.comment: Bred to appreciate further in time, but still respected on debut in an uncompetitive race

runners.name: Material Girl
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to six winners including Ken Colt (5f/5.5f including 2yo/Listed; RPR 111), Shot In The Dark (5f-7.5f including AW; 102), Acapulco Gold (5f 2yo Listed; 102) and Media Shooter (6f/7f including 2yo/AW; 93); dam Group-placed 5f winner (102); appealing pedigree and market support should be heeded.
runners.comment: Bred for speed; appealing pedigree and market support should be heeded

runners.name: I Can Dream
runners.spotlight: 20,000gns yearling; dam multiple 5f winner (including 2yo/AW; RPR 90), half-sister to winners Dont Have It Then (6f) and Clem A (1m-1m2f), out of 5f-7f winner; stable's horses usually better for a run.
runners.comment: Dam multiple 5f winner, but stable's horses are usually better for a run

runners.name: Name The Day
runners.spotlight: 15,000gns yearling; half-sister to winners Wedding Date (5f/6f including 2yo/AW; RPR 93), Amicia (French 7f; 75) and Secret Potion (5f/6f including 2yo/AW; 74); dam placed 6f AW (53), out of 7f 2yo Group 3 winner; worth a market check, but other newcomers appeal more on breeding.
runners.comment: Worth a market check, but other newcomers appeal more on breeding

runners.name: Rianka
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in both starts, over 6f at Southwell (AW) and Lingfield (good) during the spring; major improvement needed.
runners.comment: Well beaten in both starts during the spring; major improvement needed

runners.name: Ventana
runners.spotlight: Out of the frame in both starts over 7f, firstly at Newmarket (good to firm) last August and at Lingfield (AW) in January; returns from another absence, but the drop in trip may suit.
runners.comment: Returns from another absence but the drop in trip may suit

runners.name: Lady V
runners.spotlight: Well held in two starts over 6f seven months apart, the latest on stable debut at Salisbury (good) nine days ago; plenty more needed.
runners.comment: Well held in two starts over 6f seven months apart; plenty more needed

runners.name: Viennetta
runners.spotlight: Well held in both starts, at Bath (5.7f, firm) and Brighton (5f, good) for Jamie Osborne last August (hooded each time); makes her stable debut (same owner) after nine months off; probably best watched.
runners.comment: Held in both starts last August; makes stable debut after nine months off; best watched

races.race_name: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Comical Point
runners.spotlight: Useful 2yo, highly tried after his winning debut at Salisbury; third in his two runs this year, latterly in a Newmarket handicap last month (6f, good to firm); drawn wide but likely to give it another good go.
runners.comment: Useful 2yo and he has run two sound races this time around; shouldn't be far away

runners.name: Mo Of Cairo
runners.spotlight: Progressive 2yo, rounding off 2025 with a fine second at York's Ebor meeting (6f, good to firm); only sixth in his two starts this year but he has not been ridden as prominently as last year; on a good mark and a revival could be forthcoming.
runners.comment: Not matched 2yo exploits yet this year but he's handicapped to go well; revival possible

runners.name: Jel Pepper
runners.spotlight: Saw off 25 rivals when winning a 7f sales race at Newmarket last October (good ground); two defeats in Listed company this year (1m, AW and 7f, good to firm) and now drops back to 6f; well drawn for his handicap debut but this mark not an obvious gift.
runners.comment: Won big-field sales race last October; unplaced in Listed company this year; drops to 6f

runners.name: The Untamed
runners.spotlight: Confirmed the promise of his Chelmsford debut when running away with a Ripon novice in April; made hard work of defying his penalty at Doncaster 16 days ago, hanging left under pressure, but he was strong at the finish; still has potential and he's one to take seriously on this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Chasing a hat-trick after 6f wins at Ripon/Doncaster; opening mark feasible; more to come

runners.name: Amorim
runners.spotlight: His 2yo win came in good style over C&D but he was the red-hot favourite; his limitations have been exposed since and he was beaten 8l on his return to action in April (7f, good); returns to 6f with a tongue-tie and blinkers fitted; not easy to assess.
runners.comment: C&D winner last June; well beaten in two handicaps; drops in trip with accessories added
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: River Spey
runners.spotlight: Had some good form in defeat prior to landing an AW maiden last October (6f; 1-4 favourite); not at his best at Kempton in March but this mark is in range judged on his fourth at York's Ebor meeting.
runners.comment: Low-key reappearance in March; good mark judged on his 2yo best; can bounce back

runners.name: Atticum
runners.spotlight: All runs at 5f; improved with each 2yo start, winning a Yarmouth novice (good to soft) in October; tapped for speed on his seasonal/handicap debut at Sandown in April, but he was keeping on well close home and the step up to 6f could prompt further progress; ground faster than good will be a first.
runners.comment: Strong finish over 5f on seasonal/handicap debut bodes well for 6f; ground a query

runners.name: Nuevo Slovo
runners.spotlight: Good sixth in last season's Windsor Castle Stakes at Royal Ascot; 50-1 for last month's stable/seasonal/handicap debut at York and finished well beaten; dropped 4lb but not easily recommended.
runners.comment: Good run at Royal Ascot last summer; finished out the back on stable debut; best watched

runners.name: Lara Antipova
runners.spotlight: Finished second in her first two starts before readily accounting for four rivals at Lingfield seven weeks ago (6f, AW); open to improvement but it will be needed if she is to defy this mark now handicapping.
runners.comment: Ready AW success in April; unexposed but improvement essential now handicapping
runners.owner: Moulton Racing And Mrs A Jordan

runners.name: Sayidah Hard Spun
runners.spotlight: Won twice over 6f as a 2yo, including an Ascot nursery off 4lb lower; she needs to leave her Newmarket reappearance well behind if she is come out on top this time.
runners.comment: Two 6f wins as a 2yo; low-key reappearance in April; needs to take a big step forward

runners.name: Egoli
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden and a novice last summer before coming up short in stronger company; gelded over the winter; withdrawn after getting upset in the stalls at Newbury last month (6f Listed; 40-1 at the time of withdrawal); this should be more suitable but others bring stronger claims.
runners.comment: Came up short in good company after his maiden/novice wins as a 2yo; handicap debut

races.race_name: Don & Low Champions Cup 2026 Handicap

runners.name: Moon Over The Sea
runners.spotlight: Still 4lb higher than when winning over C&D (good to firm) last month, but has run well off this mark and higher in her two outings since; has finished first and second in his two starts under Myla Coppins; stable also runs Dogged; major player.
runners.comment: C&D winner last month and has remained in good form since; rider gets on well with him

runners.name: Stintino Sunset
runners.spotlight: Just 3-44 but she is consistent and has winning form over C&D; was returning from 87 days off when beaten about 4l into fourth of 11 behind Moon Over The Sea here last month and should finish closer on 5lb better terms; new headgear combination.
runners.comment: Just 3-44, but she is consistent and has winning form over C&D; new headgear combination

runners.name: Just Adair
runners.spotlight: Tongue tied all starts; won his first two handicaps, over C&D (good to firm) last August/September, and a below-par effort on her final outing was probably down to the soft ground; returns from seven months off and the market should be most informative.
runners.comment: 2-2 over C&D last year; market should be most informative after seven months off

runners.name: Guinness Lad
runners.spotlight: 0-18 but does possess ability as when second of 15 on his return from four months off here (1m2f, good to firm) nine days ago; usually takes a good grip, though, and this sort of trip has found him out in the past.
runners.comment: 0-18 but does possess ability; usually takes a good grip and still has stamina to prove

runners.name: Graffiti
runners.spotlight: Has caught the eye a couple of times recently, but isn't proving easy to win with despite a declining mark; needs first-time blinkers to make a difference.
runners.comment: Isn't proving easy to win with despite a declining mark; blinkers on

runners.name: Gert Lush
runners.spotlight: Had dropped 11lb lower than when last successful when beaten a neck at Leicester (1m2f, good to firm) last month (third and fourth have won since); races off the same mark, but she didn't get home in one previous attempt over this far.
runners.comment: Well treated and only just beaten last time, but her stamina for this far remains unproven
runners.owner: Mrs Muriel Forward & Dr G C Forward

runners.name: Launceston
runners.spotlight: 0-13 under rules; runner-up in one of his nine starts for Sir Mark Prescott, but has shown nothing in five outings for this stable, four of them over hurdles.
runners.comment: 0-13 under rules; has shown nothing in five outings for this stable, four over hurdles
runners.owner: S Keable L Cullimore G Tuck

runners.name: Regally Blonde
runners.spotlight: Drops in grade and now 2lb below her last winning mark, but well beaten in both starts since returning in April; probably needs further than this now.
runners.comment: 2lb below her last winning mark, but she probably needs further than this now
runners.owner: Case Racing Partnership & Anita J Lush

runners.name: Scammer
runners.spotlight: Huge prices and no worthwhile form in five starts over 7f-1m6f on the AW; visor given a go this time, but makes little appeal on turf debut after another 80 days off.
runners.comment: No worthwhile form in five starts on the AW; makes little appeal on turf debut; visor on

runners.name: Dogged
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner on turf, but losing run now up to 21; makes his debut for another new yard after seven months off and the market should indicate what is expected.
runners.comment: Losing run up to 21; makes stable debut after seven months off; watch market

runners.name: Lynda's Dream
runners.spotlight: Huge prices and didn't beat many in three starts over 1m/1m2f last year; makes her handicap debut after seven months off; best watched unless backed.
runners.comment: Didn't beat many in three starts last year; makes handicap debut after seven months off

races.race_name: Marley Solar Roof System "Confined" Handicap (For horses Which Have Not Won More Than One Race)

runners.name: Kalokalo
runners.spotlight: Mainly consistent since handicapping, winning at Yarmouth (1m, good to soft) last September and beaten only a neck and a nose into third of 14 at Newbury (1m2f, good) last month; major player off just 1lb higher.
runners.comment: Consistent since handicapping; beaten a neck and a nose into third at Newbury last month

runners.name: Mohaab
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark at the tenth attempt with an emphatic success at Nottingham (1m2f, good to soft) 13 days ago; 7lb rise wouldn't stop her if in the same mood, but she could probably do with rain.
runners.comment: Bolted up at Nottingham 13 days ago; 7lb higher and could probably do with rain

runners.name: My Champion
runners.spotlight: Seven starts on the AW including a narrow success at Wolverhampton (8.6f) in January, but has not matched that form in two outings in headgear since; enough to prove on turf debut.
runners.comment: Won on the AW at Wolverhampton in January, but has not matched that form since; turf debut

runners.name: Zoustellar
runners.spotlight: Responded well to cheekpieces when off the mark at Yarmouth (1m, good) in April, but disappointed at Bath (1m, firm) later in month; needs to bounce back, and her pedigree raises stamina doubts.
runners.comment: Won at Yarmouth in April, but disappointed next time; pedigree raises stamina doubts

runners.name: Bosom Pals
runners.spotlight: Narrowly made a successful nursery debut at Thirsk (7f, good to firm) last August and was returning from 254 days off when fourth of seven at Nottingham (1m, good) last month; wouldn't be sure to appreciate the longer trip on breeding.
runners.comment: May have needed last month's Nottingham return, but not sure to appreciate the longer trip

runners.name: Meritocratic
runners.spotlight: Out of the frame in four starts, failing to run up to market expectations on handicap debut at Newcastle (1m2f, AW) seven weeks ago; gelded since and a visor is now given a go; interesting to see if his backers return.
runners.comment: Out of the frame in four starts; gelded since last seen and a visor is now given a go

runners.name: Dijon Gone
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in her first three starts over 1m and finished about 4l behind Kalokalo on handicap debut at Newbury (1m2f, good) last month; needs to find a bit more.
runners.comment: Well beaten in her first three starts and about 4l behind Kalokalo at Newbury last month

runners.name: Thimble
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in three starts over 7f on the AW this year, but looks interesting up in trip for her handicap/turf debut given her stamina-laden pedigree on the dam's side; refused to enter the stalls at Nottingham last month.
runners.comment: Interesting up in trip on handicap/turf debut given stamina-laden pedigree on dam's side

runners.name: Bennyworth
runners.spotlight: All five starts on the AW, faring best when second of six on handicap debut at Lingfield (1m) in April (winner has gone in again twice since); has a bit to prove up in trip on turf debut, though.
runners.comment: Five starts on AW, finishing second of six at Lingfield in April; up in trip on turf debut

runners.name: We Ride At Dawn
runners.spotlight: Far from disgraced on her final start for Dominic Ffrench Davis at Kempton (7f, AW) last autumn, but never figured on stable/handicap debut there (1m) 12 days ago; bred to stay, but improvement is needed.
runners.comment: Never figured on stable/handicap debut at Kempton 12 days ago; improvement needed

runners.name: African Gib
runners.spotlight: Modest in three starts over 1m on turf/AW late last year, but may improve now upped in trip for his handicap debut after six months off; worth a market check.
runners.comment: May improve now upped in trip for his handicap debut after six months off

races.race_name: Skyline Roofing Centers Handicap

runners.name: Galaxy Wonder
runners.spotlight: Most consistent since joining this yard (314323112) and was only beaten half a length by Party Island in his hat-trick bid at Bath (1m2f, firm) last month; despite that latest effort a bit of rain wouldn't come amiss; stable also runs Brodie's Boy.
runners.comment: Most consistent since joining this yard (314323112), but a bit of rain wouldn't come amiss

runners.name: Hello Cotai
runners.spotlight: Three wins for Adam West including one on the AW in France; encouraging return/stable debut at Lingfield (1m2f, AW) in April but didn't build on it at Newbury (1m, good) last month; others preferred.
runners.comment: Encouraging return/stable debut at Lingfield in April, but didn't build on it next time

runners.name: Brodie's Boy
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced on his first start since wind surgery when fourth of seven at Kempton (1m3f, AW) in March, but is just 1-23 and hasn't threatened in just four outings on turf.
runners.comment: 1-23 and hasn't threatened in just four outings on turf

runners.name: Magical Merlin
runners.spotlight: Continues to edge down the weights, but losing run is up to 19 and the application of a tongue-tie made little difference last time; something to prove on his first attempt over this far.
runners.comment: Losing run up to 19 and has something to prove on his first attempt over this far

runners.name: Fast Steps
runners.spotlight: C&D winner whose losing run is up to 17 but he had dropped 14lb below his last winning mark when beaten less than a length into third of seven here four weeks ago; respected if building on that.
runners.comment: C&D winner; beaten less than a length here four weeks ago and 14lb below last winning mark

runners.name: Gretna Dreams
runners.spotlight: Won twice, over 1m2f at Brighton (good) and Newmarket (good to firm) last August, but below form in her last two outings; only 1lb lower and has fitness to prove after seven months off.
runners.comment: Won twice over 1m2f last August, but has fitness to prove after seven months off
runners.owner: J Sarkar, D Sarkar, O Sarkar & D Sarkar

runners.name: Solanna
runners.spotlight: 7-32 on the AW and 2-19 on turf, but he has performed well on grass in his last two starts including when chasing home a stablemate at Yarmouth (1m2f, good) 12 days ago after meeting trouble; should run his race.
runners.comment: 2-19 on turf, but has performed well on grass in his last two starts; should run his race

runners.name: Grey Fox
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner over 1m/1m2f here; back off his last winning mark and has twice won after an absence, but whether he can do so after 313 days off is a moot point; may also need further than this now.
runners.comment: Four-time winner over 1m/1m2f here; off 313 days and may need further than this now

runners.name: Party Island
runners.spotlight: Bids for a hat-trick after two wins at Bath (1m2f, firm), beating Galaxy Wonder by half a length on the latest occasion; up another 2lb, but still a big player in his current mood.
runners.comment: Bids for a hat-trick after two wins at Bath; up another 2lb, but still a big player

runners.name: Saliko
runners.spotlight: Last four efforts leave plenty to be desired but has dropped 2lb lower than for the latest of two wins at Nottingham (1m2f) last July; respected if the first-time cheekpieces bring about a resurgence.
runners.comment: 2lb below last winning mark; respected if first-time cheekpieces bring about a resurgence

races.race_name: Get Raceday Ready Handicap

runners.name: Rosemary's Rose
runners.spotlight: Two 1m wins on AW this spring, in a Lingfield classified event and a Kempton handicap; just respectable efforts in the meantime (slightly hampered at Bath recently) but she had excuses when last tried over 7f and might yet do better at this trip.
runners.comment: Two 1m wins on AW this spring; vulnerable since; return to 7f is worth a go

runners.name: Reputation
runners.spotlight: No sign at all of age wearying him during the winter, having won over C&D in November, and he returned from a break as fresh as paint with a 7f win at Southwell last month; back up 4lb; one of a few to consider.
runners.comment: Splendid 13yo who won over C&D in November and again at Southwell (7f) latest; considered

runners.name: Fistral Beach
runners.spotlight: Three most recent wins have come over C&D, the latest in March; has remained in form, though she bled from the nose when third at Southwell (7f) in April; if showing no ill effects she shouldn't be too far away.
runners.comment: Last three wins over C&D, the latest in March; in form but bled from the nose in April

runners.name: Thapa Vc
runners.spotlight: Multiple wins include four over C&D; his losing AW run goes back two years but he's on a handy mark now and showed he can make it pay when close up at Bath (1m) recently after getting checked in his run; blinkers return and Oisin Murphy rides for the first time; can be involved.
runners.comment: Four C&D wins; on a handy mark and checked in his run latest; O Murphy up for first time

runners.name: Fircombe Hall
runners.spotlight: C&D win was sole 7f success among eight others over 6f; however, he stayed on well and only just failed to get up over Newcastle's stiff 6f in April so a good run isn't impossible in first 7f run since March 2025.
runners.comment: Nearly all wins at 6f but has won over C&D and a good run isn't impossible back at 7f

runners.name: Amaysmont
runners.spotlight: Seven AW wins include one over C&D, and his two most recent 7f runs, here in February and at Southwell 11 days ago, were up there with his best efforts over the last year; likely to need extra from a high draw though.
runners.comment: C&D winner; latest two 7f runs up there with best recent efforts; shade more needed

runners.name: South Kensington
runners.spotlight: Won two of her last three starts for previous yard (7f; AW and turf) once tongue tied; has come to hand for new connections on AW of late, finishing well into second of 13 at Wolverhampton (about 1m) then slowly away but getting to the front quite readily at Southwell (7f) since; now 4lb higher but should have another race in her.
runners.comment: Has come to hand for new yard of late and quite cosy 7f winner on Southwell AW latest

runners.name: Lessay
runners.spotlight: Three AW wins, the latest over C&D in December; went through a quiet spell since his most recent 7f run here (in January) but fared better back over C&D in May when going close off a reduced mark; bang there.
runners.comment: C&D winner; reduced mark and back to form returned to 7f here when going close latest

runners.name: Cooramook
runners.spotlight: Has often run well on this track, including over this trip, but she often finds others are stronger finishers at 7f and has yet to break her duck; has the widest draw to overcome as well.
runners.comment: Often runs well here but not yet off the mark and others more likely for the win

runners.name: Good Karma
runners.spotlight: Things didn't pan out for him when back over 6f here in April (first-time visor; cheekpieces now refitted) but he has a good C&D record, winning once and close up in all three handicaps; has a good run in him.
runners.comment: Acts well over C&D, with a win and close up in all three handicaps; has a good run in him

runners.name: Punchbowl Flyer
runners.spotlight: Was running well at 6f and 7f here in the spring but he's never won on the AW and has seemingly gone off the boil a touch, having run a rare moderate race at Chepstow recently.
runners.comment: In good form at 6f and 7f here in the spring but may have gone off the boil now

runners.name: Happier
runners.spotlight: Both handicap wins at 5f, on Southwell AW and turf; creditable yard debut at Lingfield (6f) in December but wasn't seen again after a lesser effort that month and, having only her second 7f run, others have less to prove.
runners.comment: Off since December and has a trip query in only her second 7f start

races.race_name: Download The Raceday Ready App Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Wave Rock
runners.spotlight: Has won two of her last four races, both over this C&D, the latest off 2lb lower last month; on a career-high mark but Oisin Murphy keeps the ride and has to be considered.
runners.comment: Won two of her last four races, both over C&D; career-high mark but has to be considered

runners.name: Romantic Spirit
runners.spotlight: Has won four of 12 races on the AW, three over C&D, and the latest at Southwell last October (1m3f) off this mark; should come on for her latest run in April when back from a break; can go well.
runners.comment: 4-12 on AW, three over C&D; should improve on latest run and can go well

runners.name: Silky Lass
runners.spotlight: Won a Newcastle novice in February (1m) and sound third in an 8.5f handicap here last time; shapes as though she will stay this far (well beaten on turf when tried at 1m2f); each-way chance.
runners.comment: Newcastle novice winner (1m) who was third in a handicap here latest; each-way chance

runners.name: Pearly Squirrel
runners.spotlight: Both wins over 7f, the second here in March; good third to a subsequent winner at Newbury (1m, good) last time but not sure this step up in trip will suit as she can pull hard.
runners.comment: Won over 7f here in March; good 1m turf run latest but can pull hard so not sure to stay

runners.name: Imola
runners.spotlight: Strike-rate of 2-22 on the AW but both wins have been this year, the latest off 3lb lower over C&D in February when a well-backed favourite; has been off since so may be better for the run.
runners.comment: 2-22 on AW, both wins this year, latest over C&D in February, but has been absent since

runners.name: Quilt
runners.spotlight: Fair AW form over 7f last season; well beaten third on firm ground at Bath on handicap debut (1m); first run at this sort of trip.
runners.comment: Fair AW form last term (7f); well beaten on turf/h'cap debut (1m, firm); first try at 9.5f

runners.name: Phaedra
runners.spotlight: Stable going well and she has run two sound races on Southwell Tapeta (1m/1m3f) this spring; wouldn't rule out but has run below her best in both starts here (1m4f and 8.5f).
runners.comment: Stable going well and two good Tapeta runs this year, but below best both runs here

runners.name: Semper Femina
runners.spotlight: Unexposed; second in a Kempton maiden in March (1m3f; 9-4; winner now rated 86) but has been beaten double-digit lengths in two turf runs since; interesting on back on AW on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Good 2nd on AW on debut; well beaten on turf since; interesting back on AW for h'cap debut

runners.name: Alkumatic Jo Jo
runners.spotlight: Maiden; career-best performance when 1.75l second at Haydock last time (1m, good to soft); has some good AW form as well and comes into the reckoning but the step up a trip is a concern, having been below her best in only run at beyond 1m.
runners.comment: Maiden; good 2nd on turf last time (1m); has some sound AW runs as well; trip a concern

runners.name: La Kosmos
runners.spotlight: Three runs over 7f, all on AW, best effort when a running-on seventh at Kempton in January; likely to fare better up in trip on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Has had three runs at 7f on AW and is likely to fare better up in trip on handicap debut

runners.name: Suhub
runners.spotlight: Ex-William Haggas; still a maiden but showed a fair level of ability last year, notably when placed in novices at Leicester (7f) and Newmarket (1m), but she was well below her best in a Chelmsford AW handicap on final start (1m2f); sold 30,000gns since; acts on AW but has stamina to prove.
runners.comment: Ex-Haggas; maiden but has shown a fair level of ability including on AW; stamina to prove

races.race_name: Enjoy Summer Racing At Wolverhampton Racecourse Handicap

runners.name: Asian Journey
runners.spotlight: String of defeats at short prices this spring, remains a maiden and hasn't always looked the easiest; the return to Tapeta and step back up from 7f will help, though, and he holds claims under Tom Marquand.
runners.comment: String of defeats at short prices this spring; return to Tapeta/ step up from 7f will help

runners.name: Age Of Time
runners.spotlight: Useful in his younger days and has found his level again for Ian Williams latterly, albeit he's been expensive to follow; two wins and a close third from four runs round here and remains consistent, so enters calculations for his new yard after three months off.
runners.comment: Remains consistent and enters calculations for his new yard after three months off

runners.name: Scenario
runners.spotlight: Well held at a short price round Bath last time but paid for going with a strong pace; had hit traffic here previously and this mark should be within range for a yard also responsible for Terries Royale.
runners.comment: Excuses latest; mark should be within range for a yard also responsible for Terries Royale

runners.name: Book Of Life
runners.spotlight: Plenty of near-misses round here to go with his sole win (9.5f), and remains consistent and on a fair mark; can pull hard and may well be dropped in from stall 13, in which case he'll need some luck.
runners.comment: Remains consistent and on a fair mark; can pull hard and may need some luck from stall 13

runners.name: Hashtagnotions
runners.spotlight: Dual Newcastle winner who added two turf victories last autumn, all off lower marks; will want a good test over this trip round here and hasn't been at his best on grass lately.
runners.comment: Will want a good test over this trip round here and hasn't been at his best on turf lately

runners.name: King Of Speed
runners.spotlight: Three-time C&D winner, latterly off 4lb higher in February; fine on Polytrack but the return here is an obvious plus off a career-low mark and he's another with claims in an open handicap.
runners.comment: Three-time C&D winner, latterly off 4lb higher in February; claims in an open handicap

runners.name: Terries Royale
runners.spotlight: Dual soft-ground winner for Michael Appleby in 2024 but it's been downhill since returning from almost a year off, with plenty of poor efforts at short prices to his name; said to have been unsuited by Chepstow's undulations last time; this is easier down 3lb.
runners.comment: Said to have been unsuited by Chepstow's undulations last time; this is easier down 3lb

runners.name: Alex Belardo
runners.spotlight: Generally consistent round Dundalk in recent times, in various forms of headgear, but expensive to follow; trainer always to be respected with runners here but this is the furthest this one's been and he'll need to settle if his stamina is to hold.
runners.comment: Consistent; this is the furthest he's been so needs to settle if his stamina is to hold

runners.name: Seconds Count
runners.spotlight: Limited maiden for William Haggas and changed hands last autumn for 6,000gns; off for five months since hanging right (not for the first time) over 9.5f here for new yard and is now tried in headgear for the first time.
runners.comment: Off for five months since hanging right here for her new yard; first-time headgear on

runners.name: Luminous Warrior
runners.spotlight: Related to a few useful winners, including on Tapeta; not much has gone right since a Polytrack win over 1m in the winter and better can be expected at this lower level and back up in distance, so is one to consider.
runners.comment: Not much has gone right since a Polytrack win in the winter; one to consider

runners.name: Beaming Light
runners.spotlight: Off the boil since finishing second off a 4lb higher mark over C&D in February but he's been running in sprints latterly; this is easier than he's used to and he ought to fare much better back from a short break.
runners.comment: Running in sprints latterly; this is easier than he's used to; ought to fare much better

runners.name: Relevant Range
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed a successful winter on Tapeta at a lowly level and is back on a workable mark; again didn't look to quite see out 9.5f here three weeks ago; may well be dropped in from a wide stall.
runners.comment: Back on a workable mark; again didn't look to quite see out 9.5f here three weeks ago

runners.name: Hachiman
runners.spotlight: Wildly inconsistent for the Crisford yard and his current trainer; one of his better efforts back on turf last time but he's no certainty to build on it and his four previous efforts on Tapeta were ordinary.
runners.comment: Inconsistent; one of his better runs back on turf latest but no certainty to build on it
runners.owner: Paul K Spencer And Mrs R Carr

races.race_name: Race And Rest At The Holiday Inn Maiden Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)

runners.name: Hungarian
runners.spotlight: Finished second in a Southwell novice last July on only AW start; has since shown decent form in various company at up to 7f on turf, with both blinkers and cheekpieces tried; sets the standard but he was only third at 8-11 at Redcar last time so probably vulnerable again.
runners.comment: Second at 6f on AW on debut; has shown decent form on turf; could go well but vulnerable

runners.name: Venetian Lion
runners.spotlight: Fair 4.5l seventh of nine on Yarmouth debut (1m) but well beaten ninth of ten at Redcar last week (1m, good to firm); needs much more on AW debut.
runners.comment: Both runs at 1m; fair debut but well beaten since; needs much more on AW debut

runners.name: Ramli
runners.spotlight: Shadwell castoff by Frankel; finished third in a Huntingdon bumper (2m, good); two fair runs on the Flat since, the first when fifth of eight at Southwell (1m4f); drop in trip not sure to suit and looks one for handicaps.
runners.comment: Bumper third; 2 fair runs on the Flat (1m4f/1m2f); 8.5f not ideal; one for handicaps

runners.name: Al Qatem
runners.spotlight: By Lope De Vega; third foal of a Listed-placed 1m2f/1m4f winner (including on the AW); interesting newcomer.
runners.comment: Third foal of Listed-placed 1m2f/1m4f winner (including on the AW); interesting newcomer.

runners.name: Mithridates
runners.spotlight: 80,000euros yearling; by No Nay Never; tenth foal; half-brother to four winners at up to 2m; dam Group 2 7f-1m2f winner; worth a market check on debut.
runners.comment: Half-brother to 4 winners at up to 2m; dam 7f-1m2f winner; worth a market check on debut.

runners.name: Hozam
runners.spotlight: 32,000gns yearling who was re-sold for 67,000euros at the breeze-ups last May; twelfth foal; gelded half-brother to seven winners; an interesting newcomer.
runners.comment: 67,000euros breeze-up 2yo; half-brother to seven winners; an interesting newcomer.

runners.name: Golden Buddy
runners.spotlight: 180,000euros breeze-up buy who is a half-brother to four winners; beaten 11l in a Southwell AW maiden on debut last month (1m; 22-1); much more needed.
runners.comment: Beaten 11l in a Southwell maiden on debut last month (1m; 22-1); much more needed.

runners.name: Kaiko
runners.spotlight: Beaten 20l on last month's Bath debut (1m, firm; 100-1) and needs to make marked improvement.
runners.comment: Beaten 20l on Bath debut (1m, firm; 100-1) and needs to make marked improvement.

runners.name: Minerality
runners.spotlight: Well bred but he was a 15l eighth of 12 on Lingfield debut (AW; 50-1) last month so a lot of improvement is needed.
runners.comment: Well bred but 15l eighth on Lingfield debut (50-1) so a lot of improvement needed

runners.name: Rajiba
runners.spotlight: 70,000gns yearling by St Mark's Basilica; ran green when 2.75l fourth of eight over 6f here last November; that form fair and should improve now up in trip; each-way chance.
runners.comment: Ran green when 2.75l fourth of 8 over 6f here in November; should improve; each-way chance

runners.name: Rua Mor
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish; has shown fair form over hurdles; well beaten over C&D on stable debut last month; likely to fare better when going handicapping.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish; fair form over hurdles but well beaten over C&D on stable debut; handicap sort

races.race_name: Race And Rest At The Holiday Inn Maiden Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)

runners.name: King Of Earth
runners.spotlight: Has shown clear promise with second-place finishes at Newbury (1m, soft) last October and Doncaster (7f, good to soft) in March (winner has won again since); beaten 2l in a decent Newbury maiden (1m, good) since and this looks a good opportunity on AW debut.
runners.comment: Runner-up twice and fourth latest; this looks a good opportunity to get off the mark.

runners.name: Malika
runners.spotlight: Has shown only modest form so far but may well be capable of better; no surprise if she takes a sizeable step forward here.
runners.comment: Has shown only modest form so far but strikes as one who may be capable of better.

runners.name: Seven Sisters
runners.spotlight: Took a while to find her feet but shaped with considerable promise when runner-up in a Salisbury maiden (7f, good to firm) last month; should have come on a bundle and be suited by the step up in trip on this AW debut.
runners.comment: Ran with considerable promise when runner-up in a Salisbury maiden last month.

runners.name: Marquessofanglesey
runners.spotlight: Improved on his debut when third of nine at Southwell (7f, AW) in April; should be suited by this step back up in trip and holds place claims.
runners.comment: Improved on his debut when third at Southwell in April; this longer trip can suit
runners.owner: Julie Martin & David R Martin & Partner

runners.name: Raggaas
runners.spotlight: 200,000gns yearling; seventh foal, by St Mark's Basilica; gelded half-brother to winners Rock'n Swing (French Group-placed 7.5f 2yo/1m; RPR 102), Richmond Avenue (French Listed-placed 7f; 101) and Nova Roma (1m2f; 88); dam Group-placed 7f AW 2yo winner (98), half-sister to Dubai Millennium; sports tongue-tie on debut; betting will be best guide to his chance.
runners.comment: Well bred; sports tongue-tie on debut; betting will be best guide to his chance.

runners.name: Vino Sauro
runners.spotlight: Encouraging third on his debut for Richard Fahey in April 2025; fair third for current trainer in Meydan last November but well held on three subsequent runs there; hard to fancy on his return to Britain.
runners.comment: Fair third in Meydan last November but well held on three subsequent runs there.

runners.name: Retro Rocket
runners.spotlight: £1,800 yearling; second foal; by Rich History; dam once-raced half-sister to winners Hogmanay (4.5f/7f including 2yo/US) and Clock Tower (Italian 1m), out of 7f Group 3 winner; likely to be better for this debut run.
runners.comment: Likely to be better for this debut run

runners.name: Fiefdom
runners.spotlight: Beaten over 16l on his debut at Lingfield (7f, AW; 16-1) 13 days ago; needs to have come on a ton but may show more once he has qualified for handicaps.
runners.comment: Beaten over 16l on his debut at Lingfield 13 days ago; may do better when handicapping

runners.name: Be Hopeful
runners.spotlight: Beat only one rival on debut at Lingfield (1m2f, AW; 18-1) in April; drops back in trip and should come on for the run but may need more time.
runners.comment: Beat only one rival on his debut at Lingfield in April; may need more time

runners.name: Cy Twombly
runners.spotlight: Made the frame twice for the Crisfords in October/November 2024; gelded subsequently and changed hands for 4,800gns in March 2025; has not returned in the same form after 13 months off, including when last of 11 on handicap debut at Southwell (1m, AW; 50-1) in January, following wind surgery; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Last of 11 on handicap debut at Southwell in January; has a lot to find in a maiden

runners.name: Halimede
runners.spotlight: Always behind when ninth of 11 on debut at Southwell (1m, AW; 80-1) 11 days ago; not one for today but of more interest when she qualifies for handicaps.
runners.comment: Always behind when ninth on debut at Southwell 11 days ago; may be handicap prospect

races.race_name: Free Digital Racecard At raceday-ready.com Classified Stakes

runners.name: She's A Gift
runners.spotlight: Poor Irish maiden who's still to finish placed after 15 starts; trainer's record here down the years (11-41) entitles her to some respect on her Tapeta debut at this lower level though; interesting to see how she goes in the market.
runners.comment: Poor Irish maiden who's still to finish placed; interesting to see how she goes in market

runners.name: Blue Jay Way
runners.spotlight: Only run over 5f once, when well held over C&D two years ago, and has done most recent racing over 7f; resumed in decent form and this is easier if anything but the drop back in trip from the outside stall would have to be a worry; headgear returns.
runners.comment: This easier if anything but the drop back in trip from stall 11 would have to be a worry

runners.name: Charging Bull
runners.spotlight: Free-going maiden who bumped into one when making a lot of the running from a similar draw over C&D in April; gone both ways under pressure before but this is his first crack at 0-50 company and he's one to consider.
runners.comment: Free-going maiden; having a first crack at 0-50 company and he's one to consider

runners.name: Nad Alshiba Snow
runners.spotlight: Regressive maiden who's with her third yard; threatened to pick up a small handicap in the winter but had plenty of chances and has a bit more on her plate coming out of handicap company.
runners.comment: Had plenty of chances and has a bit more on her plate coming out of handicap company

runners.name: Colors Of Freedom
runners.spotlight: Three-time C&D winner who's well drawn for one happiest up front; only won one of her last 29 starts, which include 18 defeats at 6-1 or shorter, and she has a bit more on coming out of handicap company, but she usually gives her running round here.
runners.comment: Only won one of her last 29 starts, which include 18 defeats at 6-1 or shorter

runners.name: A Lott Of Kane
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced compared to most of these and holds obvious claims on his last-time 6f win here in March; always the concern that he'll pull hard again, from a wide stall, after two months off, but he's been found the right race.
runners.comment: Been found the right race and holds obvious claims on his last-time 6f win here in March

runners.name: She Went Whoosh
runners.spotlight: Only gone whoosh once, when winning a Polytrack handicap for Richard Hannon last spring; regressed since, often from the front, but holds claims on her latest turf second to a subsequent winner.
runners.comment: Regressed, often from the front, but claims on latest turf second to a subsequent winner

runners.name: Lion Ring
runners.spotlight: Dual course winner early in the year for 5lb claimer Ryan Kavanagh, and will be happier back on the AW and down from 7f; would have been better off with all bar one were this a handicap, however.
runners.comment: Be happier back on the AW and down from 7f; bit to find on these terms though

runners.name: He's An Angel
runners.spotlight: Not as good since missing more than a year, does most racing over 6f and will always come with risks, given a propensity to blow the start and/or pull hard; this is easier down to 0-50 company for the first time, though, and he's capable if it falls right.
runners.comment: Will always come with risks, given a propensity to blow the start and pull but this easier

runners.name: Yesnia
runners.spotlight: Three ordinary efforts round Dundalk at huge prices last backend; offered more for her new yard, minus the hood and in a tongue-tie, when not best drawn as it turned out on her turf/handicap debut, in April; could have improvement in her.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish maiden; bit more for her new yard in April; could have improvement in her

runners.name: Bust A Moon
runners.spotlight: Won a Beverley seller last May but then struggled for two yards; this easier for another new stable and he ran well first time out last season, which gives hope after eight months off.
runners.comment: Won Beverley seller last May but then struggled for two yards; this a bit easier on return

races.race_name: Get The Inside Track With raceday-ready.com Handicap

runners.name: Initial Blue
runners.spotlight: Enjoying a consistent year, winning twice from the front at Kempton since the blinkers went on (doesn't have to lead); nothing wrong with his clear second at Southwell a month ago and holds decent claims despite a further 2lb rise.
runners.comment: Nothing wrong with his latest clear second; holds decent claims despite a further 2lb rise

runners.name: Watermelon Sugar
runners.spotlight: Took advantage of a sliding mark round Lingfield in the winter (last four wins come there but is fine on Tapeta); off the boil since and this is a bit tougher; would want to see some support tonight.
runners.comment: Took advantage of sliding mark at Lingfield in the winter; would want to see some support

runners.name: Teardrops
runners.spotlight: Three Newcastle wins for Richard Spencer over the winter, latterly off 5lb lower, and remained competitive before changing hands for 5,000gns; usual tongue-tie/blinkers absent for his new yard latest (same again here), when well below par on turf.
runners.comment: Usual tongue-tie/blinkers absent for his new yard latest, when well below par on turf

runners.name: Winchurch
runners.spotlight: Took advantage of a sliding mark when easily winning an ordinary Nottingham handicap from the front five weeks ago; drawn to attack again and fine on Tapeta but an 8lb rise will test him into a deeper race.
runners.comment: Drawn to attack again and fine on Tapeta but an 8lb rise will test him into a deeper race
runners.owner: SBK Racing & B & J Parr Racing Ltd

runners.name: Iconic Times
runners.spotlight: Wins for Heather Main came at Kempton; not at his best for this yard, including back to sprinting last time after a spell over further, and isn't obviously fancied from the outside stall.
runners.comment: Not at his best for this yard and isn't obviously fancied from the outside stall

runners.name: Daytona Lady
runners.spotlight: Can miss the break and is often ridden from off the pace (every chance she will be again from stall 12); notched a third C&D win when taking a small-field affair against her own sex off 2lb lower in April; slight drop-off latest and has to bounce back.
runners.comment: Can miss the break and often ridden patiently; slight drop-off latest; has to bounce back
runners.owner: Eric Broadwith And Mrs Ruth Carr

runners.name: Giorgio M
runners.spotlight: Three wins, and most racing, come over 7f, including when making all on his second start for this yard on Polytrack off 6lb higher in the winter; not at his best lately and, while well drawn, may not get his own way as he likes back down in trip.
runners.comment: Three wins and most racing come over 7f; may not get his own way as he likes down in trip

runners.name: Fuji Mountain
runners.spotlight: Decent 2yo who proved hard to place last year and has again lacked consistency this spring; the blinkers come off as he makes his AW debut off a career-low mark and it's hard to know what to expect in truth.
runners.comment: Decent 2yo who proved hard to place last year and has again lacked consistency this spring

runners.name: Havana Sky
runners.spotlight: All eight wins come over this trip, including three over C&D, but never off a mark quite this high; nothing to suggest he's best caught fresh but the booking of Oisin Murphy is an obvious plus after three months off.
runners.comment: All eight wins come over this trip, including three over C&D; Oisin Murphy an obvious plus

runners.name: Lesley's Boy
runners.spotlight: Decent 2yo who struggled last year, coming more than a stone down the weights; found his level again latterly on Tapeta but he ran poorly on his turf comeback in April and others make more obvious appeal.
runners.comment: Found his level again latterly on Tapeta but he ran poorly on his turf comeback in April

runners.name: Beyond Borders
runners.spotlight: Currently 2-3 on Tapeta but he's generally struggled since leaving Karl Burke, his only win coming when allowed his own way in a small field last summer (been dropped in lately); the first-time tongue-tie will need to make a difference.
runners.comment: Generally struggled since leaving Karl Burke; wears first-time tongue-tie

runners.name: Wodao
runners.spotlight: Listed winner in Ireland as a 2yo who regressed sharply for Jamie Osborne; been off for four four months since well held over C&D and the change of scenery will need to have done the trick.
runners.comment: Listed winner in Ireland as a 2yo who regressed for Jamie Osborne; off four four months

races.race_name: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap

runners.name: Virtue Patience
runners.spotlight: Better record on Polytrack than turf and the handicapper's given him every chance, with him now 3lb lower than when not beaten far as favourite over C&D in January; looks set to go well back on the AW.
runners.comment: 3lb lower than when not beaten far over C&D in January; set to go well back on the AW

runners.name: Amber Honey
runners.spotlight: Hold-up filly whose win for Ed Dunlop, from whom she was picked up in April for 7,500gns, came on Tapeta; become hard to predict and there's no sign of the usual headgear back from a short break.
runners.comment: Become hard to predict and there's no sign of the usual headgear for her new yard

runners.name: Showmedemoney
runners.spotlight: Goes back to the blinkers worn for both Newcastle wins, latterly off 3lb lower, for Iain Jardine in the winter but again no sign of the usual tongue-tie; big price and well held for his new yard on turf a few weeks ago.
runners.comment: Again no sign of the tongue-tie; big price and well held for new yard on turf last time

runners.name: Pitney
runners.spotlight: Rated much higher when joining this yard in autumn 2024; remained consistent enough off a sliding mark during the winter but needs more if he's to snap the losing run.
runners.comment: Consistent off a sliding mark during the winter; needs more if he's to snap the losing run

runners.name: Play Me
runners.spotlight: Epsom winner last August for regular partner Donagh Murphy off a 5lb lower mark; fair comeback over C&D behind Buy The Dip in April (6lb better off today) but didn't build on that back on turf a fortnight ago.
runners.comment: Fair comeback over C&D behind Buy The Dip in April; didn't build on it back on turf latest
runners.owner: P And Mrs L Rowe And John Turner

runners.name: Volto Di Medusa
runners.spotlight: Tapeta win last summer came off this mark and the booking of Rossa Ryan is a positive as he returns to the AW after three ordinary efforts on turf; not always looked straightforward and interesting to see whether support comes this time.
runners.comment: Not always looked straightforward; interesting to see whether support comes this time

runners.name: Searchingtheblues
runners.spotlight: Won her first two as a 2yo and entered handicaps on an unrealistic mark; best efforts of late have come on Tapeta but she's won on Polytrack and has been offering more of late; capable at this level.
runners.comment: Won on Polytrack before and has been offering more of late; capable at this level

runners.name: Secret Road
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed a revival for James Owen and this yard earlier in the year, with three wins, including two round here; up in the weights now, though, and finished well behind a couple of these over C&D a month ago; needs more from somewhere.
runners.comment: Well behind a couple of these over C&D a month ago; needs more from somewhere

runners.name: Victory Sound
runners.spotlight: Useful 2yo for Charles Hills who struggled badly during a limited campaign last season; handicapper quick to ease off and it wouldn't be the biggest shock were he to pop up for a yard also responsible for Searchingtheblues.
runners.comment: Handicapper quick to ease off and it wouldn't be the biggest shock were he to pop up

runners.name: Dios De La Guerra
runners.spotlight: Regressive maiden who had breaks between his three handicaps runs last year (beaten a long way each time); the first-time headgear will need to be a difference maker back from six months off.
runners.comment: Regressive maiden; first-time headgear has to make a difference back from six months off

runners.name: Buy The Dip
runners.spotlight: Well suited by switchback tracks and took advantage of a handy mark when winning on his C&D comeback in April; found life tougher off his revised mark in better company here last time but this is more his speed back down 2lb.
runners.comment: Won on his C&D comeback in April; this is more his speed back down 2lb; claims

runners.name: Mbappe
runners.spotlight: Two turf wins here and two Polytrack victories at Kempton, latterly off 3lb lower in January; below-par in first-time cheekpieces (retained) when last seen four months ago (won fresh before) but remains on a workable mark.
runners.comment: Below-par in first-time cheekpieces (retained) when last seen; remains on a workable mark

races.race_name: Download The At The Races App Handicap

runners.name: Tomarlo
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish gelding who struggled in three runs early in the year for Barry Brennan; well backed for his new yard on Tapeta early last month, when things didn't pan out his way, and is on an attractive mark currently; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Well backed for new yard latest, when things didn't pan out his way; on an attractive mark

runners.name: Top Star
runners.spotlight: Longstanding maiden who missed a chunk of his career; tried in three forms of headgear this year, including when fourth behind two of these over C&D a month ago, and is given another try without today.
runners.comment: Longstanding maiden who missed a chunk of his career; tried without headgear today

runners.name: Ashford Hill
runners.spotlight: Winner here early in the year who's been held a few times since; running in a sprint for just the second time and, for all that she's a keen-goer, whether she'll have the required toe round here is the question.
runners.comment: In a sprint for just the second time; whether she'll have the toe round here the question

runners.name: Griselda
runners.spotlight: Regressive and inconsistent for this yard, and is tried in a first-time visor instead of cheekpieces tonight; needs to bounce back from a couple of ordinary efforts on turf.
runners.comment: Regressive and inconsistent; has to bounce back from a couple of ordinary efforts on turf

runners.name: Poetic Jack
runners.spotlight: Pulled hard when well behind three of these as favourite over C&D a month ago; another poor one on turf since tempers enthusiasm.
runners.comment: Pulled hard when well behind three of these as favourite over C&D a month ago

runners.name: Jackson Street
runners.spotlight: Regressive gelding who's having his first run in a sprint since this time last year and is drawn widest; well held back from a break on turf here a fortnight ago (7f) but will be happier back on Polytrack should they go hard in front.
runners.comment: Regressive gelding; be happier back on Polytrack should they go hard in front

runners.name: Southbank
runners.spotlight: Nothing wrong with his C&D second a month ago and was unlucky back on turf last time (same Bath handicap he won last season), when hitting plenty of traffic; will rightly be popular.
runners.comment: Unlucky back on turf last time, when hitting plenty of traffic; will rightly be popular

runners.name: Chasing Gold
runners.spotlight: Maiden who's had his moments at the start and often pulled hard; reared as the stalls opened on his turf comeback and unseated, so can only be considered risky in first-time blinkers.
runners.comment: Reared as the stalls opened on turf comeback and unseated; can only be considered risky

runners.name: Walking On Clouds
runners.spotlight: Missed a chunk of last year and has slid to a competitive mark for his current yard; just behind Southbank over C&D a month ago and is one to consider back in headgear (second-time blinkers).
runners.comment: Slid to a competitive mark for his current yard; one to consider back in headgear

runners.name: Dreamcrafter
runners.spotlight: Achieved little in a handful of starts last year (5f-7f) and is hard to fancy returning from a lengthy absence.
runners.comment: Achieved little in a handful of starts last year; hard to fancy returning from an absence

races.race_name: Follow @AtTheRaces On X Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Parisian Scholar
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who landed three 1m handicaps last autumn, the last two at Newcastle (Tapeta); twice placed off 2lb higher at Kempton (1m3f, Polytrack) earlier this year, but may be vulnerable to an improver; visor replaces cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Twice placed at Kempton earlier this year, but may be vulnerable to an improver; visor on

runners.name: Starship Trooper
runners.spotlight: Has shown ability and was only just behind Parisian Scholar when fourth of five on handicap debut at Kempton (1m3f, Polytrack) in April; might not come into his own until contesting a strongly run race in a bigger field, though.
runners.comment: Has shown ability, but might not come into his own until contesting a strongly run race

runners.name: Astrological
runners.spotlight: Both starts at Wolverhampton (9.5f, Tapeta), finishing second of five on debut in March before landing odds of 1-2 the following month; that latest form hasn't worked out, but he remains unexposed and the longer trip shouldn't be an issue on this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Second and first in two starts at Wolverhampton; longer trip shouldn't be an issue

runners.name: Wardlaw
runners.spotlight: Fourth of 13 behind Talk Of New York on his third start at Kempton (1m, Polytrack) last October, but having been gelded he looked one-paced when fifth of seven on handicap/turf debut at Windsor (11.4f, good) a fortnight ago; needs to improve for the cheekpieces, but that is possible.
runners.comment: One-paced on handicap debut at Windsor a fortnight ago, but could improve; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Charles Darnay
runners.spotlight: Built on earlier promise upped in trip when off the mark in a Kempton maiden (1m4f, Polytrack) in March; off three months and this easier track may not be ideal, but the three immediately behind him at Kempton have all won since; respected on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Off the months since winning at Kempton, but that form has been franked; respected

races.race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Mrair
runners.spotlight: Turned over at short odds on debut when runner-up at Newmarket (6f, good) 17 days ago where the Dip looked to put him out; nevertheless he still caught the eye and may warrant another chance with Ryan Moore on board.
runners.comment: Turned over at short odds on debut when runner-up at Newmarket; deserves another chance

runners.name: Storm Blade
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; by Night Of Thunder; brother to 6f/1m AW winner Storm Star (RPR 106), half-brother to 6f 2yo winner Planet Seeker (80); dam 6f/7f winner (including 2yo/Group 3; 113), half-sister to winners Foxtrot Lady (7f Group 3) and Dance Of Fire (useful 1m-1m2f including 2yo); yard going well and no surprise were he to feature on this debut.
runners.comment: Smart pedigree; yard going well and no surprise were he to feature on this debut.

runners.name: Rapid Deployment
runners.spotlight: 42,000gns yearling; first foal; by Zoustar; dam multiple 5f AW winner (including 2yo; RPR 104), half-sister to winners No Jet Lag (US 1m Grade 2) and Optari (French Group-placed 6f 2yo), out of 6f 2yo Listed winner; yard going well and 3-8 with their juveniles this year; likely contender on this debut.
runners.comment: Yard going well and 3-8 with their juveniles this year; market can guide

runners.name: Adeel
runners.spotlight: £42,500 yearling, £70,000 breeze-up 2yo; third foal; by Havana Grey; brother to 6f winner Tawasol (RPR 97); dam 6f winner (including 2yo/AW; 78), half-sister to 6f Group 3 winner Tabdeed; yard among the winners recently but this looks a hot contest in which to make his debut.
runners.comment: Yard among the winners recently but this looks a hot contest in which to make debut

runners.name: Final Objective
runners.spotlight: 55,000euros yearling, £115,000 breeze-up 2yo; third foal; by Sioux Nation; closely related to 5f/6f AW winner Midnight Call (RPR 69); dam once-raced close relative of 6f 2yo Listed winner Cape Factor, half-sister to winners My Eyes Adore You (6f Listed) and Court House (smart 1m-1m4f); has a speedy pedigree and worth a market check; wears hood for debut.
runners.comment: Has a speedy pedigree and worth a market check; wears hood for debut

runners.name: Rattenbury
runners.spotlight: 62,000euros foal, 75,000euros yearling; fifth foal; Blackbeard half-brother to 6f AW winner Dyed In The Wool (RPR 74); dam 5f 2yo winner (76), half-sister to four winners out of US 6f stakes winner; not ruled out on debut but he may well improve for the outing.
runners.comment: Not ruled out on debut but he may well improve for the outing.

runners.name: Rogue Imperial
runners.spotlight: Caught the eye on debut when fifth of eight at Yarmouth (6f, good; 3-1) 12 days ago when, after travelling well, he looked to blow up; should benefit from the run and ought to go well.
runners.comment: Caught the eye on debut when fifth at Yarmouth 12 days ago; open to improvement

runners.name: Blue Skies Above
runners.spotlight: Beat only one home on his debut at Kempton (6f, AW; 11-2) last month; makes turf debut and needs to have come on leaps and bounds.
runners.comment: Beat only one home on his debut at Kempton last month.

races.race_name: Follow @AtTheRaces On Instagram Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: The Bureau Club
runners.spotlight: Habitual slow starter whose best effort by some way was when fourth in a Kempton maiden in March (1m); lesser efforts either side of that, including on turf debut at Salisbury last time (7f, good to firm; beaten 11.5l); bit to prove.
runners.comment: Habitual slow starter; best run when 4th at Kempton (1m); lesser run on turf debut latest

runners.name: Perola
runners.spotlight: Placed on three of his four runs, last time finishing third over 7f at Wolverhampton; has since had wind surgery; interesting, but was below par only previous run on turf.
runners.comment: Placed on three of his 4 starts (3rd at Wolverhampton latest); below-par only turf start

runners.name: Star Banner
runners.spotlight: Second foal of a 6f juvenile winner; shaped with promise when third in a Musselburgh novice 11 days ago (7f, good); bit green that day so should fare even better this time.
runners.comment: Promising 3rd in a Musselburgh novice 11 days ago (7f, good); improvement likely; chance

runners.name: Chasse Patate
runners.spotlight: Made late headway to take third in a Kempton novice on debut 12 days ago (7f; 28-1, Santiago Boy back in fifth); should improve; one to consider.
runners.comment: Running-on third at Kempton on debut 12 days ago; should improve; one to consider

runners.name: Kennington
runners.spotlight: Creditable third in a 1m maiden on the AW here in April; should improve on that and worth considering.
runners.comment: Creditable third in an AW maiden (1m) here on debut; should improve and worth considering
runners.owner: Mrs M T Bevan & A Bevan

runners.name: Santiago Boy
runners.spotlight: Improved on his debut run when 3l fifth in a Kempton novice last time (7f, standard to slow; 125-1, bit to find with third-placed Chasse Patate); further progress likely; each-way chance.
runners.comment: Improved on debut when 3l fifth at Kempton latest; should improve again; each-way chance

runners.name: Mythological Star
runners.spotlight: 19,000euros yearling; didn't show a great deal on last month's Kempton debut (66-1) but is a likely improver.
runners.comment: Didn't show a great deal on last month's Kempton debut (66-1) but is a likely improver

runners.name: Ocean Force
runners.spotlight: Well beaten all starts so far, last time finishing 16l fifth of nine in a C&D novice; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Well beaten on his three starts to date (tailed off over C&D latest); hard to fancy

races.race_name: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Handicap

runners.name: Tattie Bogle
runners.spotlight: Has generally been consistent this year, winning for the first time on turf at Musselburgh (7f, good) in April; recorded a rare blip at Leicester last month but back on track when runner-up at Ayr (7f, good to firm) 12 days ago; respected.
runners.comment: Won for the first time on turf at Musselburgh in April; runner-up at Ayr 12 days ago.

runners.name: Portman Blue
runners.spotlight: Lightly-raced but beat only one home at this track (7f, AW) in April on his handicap debut; a first-time tongue-tie is fitted and still has time on his side.
runners.comment: Lightly-raced but beat only one home at this track in April on his handicap debut.

runners.name: Rogue Dynasty
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark on her stable debut at Chester (7f, good) last July and has performed with credit on each run since bar a poor effort on soft at Sandown in August; should be better for her reappearance at this track (1m, AW) when fourth of six in March and holds claims in first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Should be better for her reappearance at this track when fourth in March.

runners.name: Ironist
runners.spotlight: Consistent sort who got off the mark at Yarmouth (6f, soft) off 2lb lower last September; respectable efforts since and if the rain arrives then that will enhance her claims.
runners.comment: Consistent sort who got off the mark at Yarmouth off 2lb lower last September.

runners.name: Amused
runners.spotlight: Ex-French gelding who was unraced at two; made all to land Vichy claimer (very soft) in August and went mighty close for this yard in Leicester seller (good) in October; 25-1 and raced wide before solid fifth of 12 at Kempton (also 7f, AW) in December; showed no improvement in first-time cheekpieces when last of ten on his return at Epsom (8.5f, good) in April and visor returns; best left alone at present.
runners.comment: Won when trained in France last August; finished last on return in April.
runners.owner: P And Mrs L Rowe And John Turner

runners.name: Dandy G Boy
runners.spotlight: Won twice on AW for this yard last year; yet to be placed from five runs on turf and runs off his highest mark; may be better for the run.
runners.comment: Won twice on AW for this yard last year; yet to be placed from five runs on turf.

runners.name: H Key Lails
runners.spotlight: Races mainly on AW where he has six wins; 1-15 in this sphere and may be worth waiting until he returns to AW.
runners.comment: 1-15 in this sphere and may be worth waiting until he returns to AW (six wins).

runners.name: Big Alex Walmsley
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced but well held in handicaps on his last two runs; has been gelded since and returns from a break of 236 days; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Lightly raced but well held in handicaps on his last two runs.

runners.name: Dutch Kingdom
runners.spotlight: All seven wins have been on AW; yet to hit form in two runs for this yard; cheekpieces return but best watched at present.
runners.comment: All seven wins have been on AW; best watched at present.
